description: docs/runtime-contract.md has a transport-agnostic contract gap between Capability 3 (record routing) and the shell-hook-only transport description. Capability 3 states: "The runtime never writes `records/**`, `meta-state.jsonl`, or `runtime-state.jsonl` directly. All writes go through the loop's tools, so the registry's invariants (4-kind union, status lifecycle, grounding fingerprints) hold regardless of which runtime produced the record." The shell-hook-only transport description states: "only the gate hooks are wired (no MCP tool surface); the runtime relies on the loop's file-based records **without a tool channel**." These two statements tension: if there is no tool channel, how do writes satisfy Capability 3? Two readings surfaced in plans/reports/from-root-cause-to-transport-decision-260711-0011-mcp-stateless-adapter-vs-cli-report.md §7: (a) shell-hook-only is read-only participation (capability 3 is unsatisfiable under that transport, contract should say so); (b) a CLI counts as a tool channel (just not MCP), so the contract should name it and wiring a CLI would *define* the shell-hook-only transport rather than merely wire it. Unresolved since plan 260711-0011 (Unresolved Question #2 in the prior decision report). This is a pre-existing L2 design question; the current plan 260711-0030 (stateless MCP adapter) does NOT wire shell-hook-only and explicitly defers the question. Decision needed before any non-MCP transport is wired (library-import or CLI). Deferred to follow-up plan per the prior report's recommendation. Scope: contract clarification + optional transport definition.

tools/learning-loop-mastra/__tests__/legacy-mcp/lifecycle-migration-finalize.test.js contained hard-coded snapshot count assertions tied to a specific migration event: `>= 22 open findings` (the migration flipped 10 active + 12 stale → open) and `>= 229 total entries` (the pre-migration registry size). Both broke under concurrent MCP-session activity (the test failed 2026-07-11 when 2 migrated findings got resolved by other sessions, dropping the open count from 22 to 21 — the underlying H7 cross-process race the stateless MCP adapter plan 260711-0030 addresses). Refactored the test to remove the redundant `>= 22` (covered by 0-active + 0-stale structural invariants) and the unrecoverable `>= 229` (no-losses cannot be expressed without migration markers). The deeper schema problem remains: future migrations cannot express structural post-migration invariants because the registry has no migration_target + pre_count + post_count fields on the migration's change-log entry. Proposed fix: when a migration runs a `meta_state_batch` that flips many entries at once, the migration should write a separate change-log entry with `applies_to.migration: { target: 'X', pre_count: {finding: 22, total: 229}, post_count: {finding: 22, total: 229}, idempotency: 'sha256-of-mutation' }`. Post-migration tests then assert: `meta_state_list({entry_kind: 'change-log', ref_by: 'migration-target-X'})` returns exactly the expected count; the migration's affected entries are tagged in their `change_diff.migration_target` field and can be enumerated for post-hoc structural checks. Today's finding is open; ships when the next migration lands.
resolution: Closed by plan 260712-0300 Phase 2: `operation_envelope` field on change-log entries lets tests assert exact pre/post counts via the structured envelope (red-team finding 7 fix — rejected loose-bound rewrite). The legacy `lifecycle-migration-finalize.test.js` brittle count assertions were already removed per meta-state.jsonl:271; this plan adds a NEW forward-looking assertion (`(h-fresh-assertion) deterministic fixture: exact deepEqual on pre/post counts`) in `change-log-operation-envelope.test.js` against a 22-entry deterministic fixture. content_hash replaces `idempotency` as the field name (red-team finding 4 — content-hash semantics, not replay detection). Change-log refs: meta-260712T0437Z-... (batch integration + auto-emit) and meta-260712T0438Z-... (IMMUTABLE_PATCH_FIELDS extension + write-path reject).

title: assertinvariant: core-logic owns its invariant
description: Boundary helper at tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/meta-state.js. Wraps any operation that owns an invariant and returns structured failure shape when violated. Replaces 5+ ad-hoc fail-loud mechanisms (assertWriteVisible, isSchemaBranchSupported, withRegistryLock, inbound-gate markers, pre-commit stderr summary) with one contract. 5 call sites: file-readers.js#L10 (unmapped active entries), meta-state-report-tool.js (id honoring), pre-commit consistency check (stderr summary), meta-state-batch (migration markers), tools rm consult-gate (import-chain).

title: change-log migration markers: target + pre/post counts + idempotency
description: Schema enhancement: when a migration runs a meta_state_batch that flips many entries at once, accept migration_target + pre_count + post_count + idempotency. Auto-emit a marker change-log entry alongside the batch. Post-migration tests assert structural invariants via the markers instead of raw counts (closes the test-fragility shape captured in meta-260711T0144Z). Sub-piece of loop-design-assertinvariant-core-logic-invariant-wrapper — one of the 5 call sites the assertinvariant primitive enables.

title: change-log operation envelope: kind + pre/post counts + idempotency for batch mutations
description: Schema enhancement on change-log entries: top-level `operation_envelope` field with kind (migration|sweep|closeout|consolidation|backfill|archive-wave|escalation-batch|manual-batch), target (operation identifier), pre_count + post_count (each {total, by_status:{open,active,stale,resolved,superseded}, by_kind:{finding,change-log,rule,loop-design}}), and idempotency (sha256 hash). Captures magnitude of any batch mutation alongside the existing identity-level change_diff and consolidates fields. Supersedes loop-design-migration-markers-on-change-log (inactive, status flipped this session) which scoped the field to migrations only — broadened to 8 batch-mutation kinds per the user's scope-expansion request. Use cases: migrations (status flips), drift-driven closeouts (e.g. meta-260710T0152Z which resolved 3 + modified 7), consolidations (e.g. meta-260711T0337Z), TTL sweeps, backfills (e.g. SP2 grounding migration), archive waves, escalation batches, manual batches. New helper at core/operation-envelope.js owns envelope construction/validation so batch handlers emit a structured envelope rather than free-form change_diff arrays.

`meta_state_patch` corrupts `entry_kind` on existing loop-design entries when given an empty or non-kind-specific patch. Repro: this session patched loop-design-migration-markers-on-change-log (entry_kind stored as 'loop-design') via `meta_state_patch({id, entry_kind: 'loop-design', patch: {}})` — the result was the entry's stored entry_kind flipped to 'finding' (and version bumped). Same bug hit loop-design-assertinvariant-core-logic-invariant-wrapper in the same session.

**Root cause:** the patch tool's `patch` param is `z.union(PATCH_KINDS.map(k => buildPatchSchemaFor(k)))` (meta-state-patch-tool.js:23). An empty patch `{}` validates against the FIRST union member (finding) because all 4 per-kind schemas are `.partial().strict()`. The finding schema has `entry_kind: z.literal('finding').default('finding')` (meta-state.js:89), so Zod's parse applies the default and emits `{entry_kind: 'finding'}`. Then `Object.assign(entry, cleanPatch)` at meta-state.js:646 writes that into the stored entry.

**Affected entries (corrupted this session):**
- loop-design-assertinvariant-core-logic-invariant-wrapper — stored entry_kind='finding', should be 'loop-design'
- loop-design-migration-markers-on-change-log — stored entry_kind='finding', should be 'loop-design'

loop-design-operation-envelope-on-change-log was created cleanly via propose_design and is unaffected.

**Blocker:** the patch tool is now also unable to FIX these entries because line 43 (`if (entry.entry_kind !== entry_kind)`) refuses when stored ≠ expected. Direct file writes to meta-state.jsonl are blocked by the write gate. The two corrupted loop-designs are functionally invisible to filters keyed on entry_kind (loop_describe's active_finding_count, meta_state_list with entry_kind filter, etc.).

**Recovery paths (operator decision):**
1. Operator-mediated direct file edit on meta-state.jsonl (requires operator preflight marker — gates normally block this).
2. New MCP tool `meta_state_repair_entry_kind` that explicitly patches entry_kind (gated, audited).
3. Fix the patch tool itself (prevent empty patches from writing defaults) and run a repair script that re-asserts entry_kind from the per-kind schemas' `.literal()` values.

**Recommended fix to the patch tool:** in `updateEntry` at meta-state.js:642-646, drop `entry_kind` from `cleanPatch` unless it was in the user's raw patch. The per-kind schema's `.default()` was a convenience that became a footgun under the union-of-parts patch schema.

**Relationship to the meta-pattern:** this is another instance of the silent-success / silent-corruption pattern captured in loop-design-assertinvariant-core-logic-invariant-wrapper. An `assertinvariant` wrapper around `updateEntry` that asserts `entry.entry_kind` is unchanged after the patch would catch this exact bug. Filing this finding as an instance of the assertinvariant meta-pattern.
resolution: Closed by plan 260712-0724 Phase 1: assertinvariant is the universal pre-state-only wrapper applied at writeEntry, updateEntry, archiveEntry, deleteEntry, and metaStateBatch boundaries. The patch-tool corruption class is closed by the wrapper at writeEntry + the kept delete cleanPatch.entry_kind defense (line 710) + the kept IMMUTABLE_PATCH_FIELDS deny-list (lines 339-355). The deny-list is NOT removed (Red Team Finding 2: patch-tool has its own handler-side deny-list that fires BEFORE updateEntry's mutation).

title: assertinvariant: universal scope, every core-logic operation
description:
Canonical version of the assertinvariant primitive with universal scope (per operator direction this session). Scope is EVERY core-logic operation that owns an invariant the agent depends on — not a curated subset. Narrow scopes are hand-wavy and miss the next domain (this session proved it 3 times: narrow design 2 to migrations → broadened to 8 kinds; narrow v1 to write-tools+file-readers → broadened to include updateEntry/archiveEntry/deleteEntry after the patch-tool corruption bug hit; narrow patch-tool fix to drop entry_kind from cleanPatch → broadened to the universal identity-invariant wrapper).

**Why universal scope:** curating the call site list produces hand-wavy fixes that miss the next instance. This session is the proof — three narrow-scoped attempts, three misses. The primitive's cost is uniform; its benefit compounds with every call site. Operator direction: "if not universal, the agent will hand-wavy".

**Wrapper primitive:** `assertinvariant(operation, {accept, returnOnFail, logTo})` at `core/operation-invariant.js` (separate file, not co-located with meta-state.js — separation of concerns). Returns structured failure shape when violated.

**Seed call sites (5+ observed, universal scope means future sites are wrapped by default):**
1. `core/file-readers.js#L10` — unmapped active entries (finding #6)
2. `tools/handlers/meta-state-report-tool.js` — input id honoring (finding #3)
3. `hooks/universal/pre-commit` — consistency check stderr summary (finding #5)
4. `tools/handlers/meta-state-batch-tool.js` — operation envelope (design 3; finding #7)
5. `tools rm` consult-gate — import-chain analysis (finding #1)
6. **`core/meta-state.js#updateEntry`** — identity-invariant (this session's bug, finding meta-260712T0053Z-...)
7. **`core/meta-state.js#archiveEntry`** — identity-invariant (latent — same bug class)
8. **`core/meta-state.js#deleteEntry`** — identity-invariant (latent — same bug class)

**Companion rule:** `rule-assertinvariant-at-boundary` — agent-side consult that fires when a new core-logic operation is added. Fires on regex `export function (writeEntry|updateEntry|archiveEntry|deleteEntry|...)` matches. Without this rule, the universal scope still applies but the agent has to remember to wrap; with it, the loop reminds.

**Canonical replacement for:** loop-design-assertinvariant-core-logic-invariant-wrapper (corrupted this session, will be repaired by operator). When that design is repaired, this design flips to status=inactive via supersede.

.github/workflows/test.yml pins fallow-rs/fallow@<sha> with version resolved from package.json devDependencies.fallow (currently v2.102.0). Local fallow binary is v3.3.0. The two versions emit materially different `audit` verdicts for the same diff: v2.102.0 reports `ISSUES: 3, VERDICT: fail` for the 3 PR-#53 gate failures (registry-writes.js unused-files, REGISTRY_FILENAME unused-exports, 8-line × 3-pair duplication), while v3.3.0 against commit aaf9995 reports `dead_code_introduced: 0, duplication_introduced: 0` for the same fix and instead attributes 1 pre-existing handler complexity as `complexity_introduced: 1`. v3.3.0 also follows `#lib/*` npm-import aliases across the analysis-root boundary (it sees tools/lib/gate-logging.js exports as unused, which v2.102.0 ignores). Local `pnpm fallow:gate` is therefore not a faithful predictor of the CI gate — passing locally may still fail on CI (or vice versa). Fix: bump package.json devDependencies.fallow, fallow-rs/fallow@<sha> pin, and version-specific flags (--coverage path, --dupes-mode mild) to a v3.x release that matches the local binary; re-baseline baselines/fallow/*.json against the new analyzer since analysis rules differ between majors.
resolution: Aligned CI to v3.3.0 to match mise-fallow debug binary. Bumped devDependencies.fallow 2.102.0→3.3.0, fallow-rs/fallow@<sha> 7ec8073…→c9326d9… (v3.3.0 tag), and refreshed the resolve-comment block in test.yml. pnpm install regenerated pnpm-lock.yaml (74 lines); both binaries now report `fallow 3.3.0` verified. v3 reports 3 NEW INTRODUCED false-positives (1 unused-file + 2 unused-exports) that v2 missed: (a) __tests__/helpers/manifest-constants.cjs — added __tests__/helpers/**/*.cjs to .fallowrc.json dynamicallyLoaded (test files require it; fallow ignores *.test.cjs so the requires are invisible); (b) tools/lib/gate-logging.js appendGateLog + logToolCall — added to ignoreExports (re-exports of canonical tools/lib/gate-logging.js outside fallow root; #lib/* alias consumers are out of scope). Re-baselined dead-code/health/dupes against v3 with the suppressions active. Verified pnpm fallow:gate ✓ pass (45 changed files, 0 issues), pnpm test 1840/1840 pass, pnpm test:cold-session 11/11 pass, workflow-shape.test.js 17/17 pass (SHA-pin regex accepts new 40-char hex). Two soft warnings remain on local-only (do not gate-block): node_modules directory not found (v3 looks at a different path than v2 on monorepo layouts) and "duplication baseline has 7 entries but matched 0 current clone groups" (v3 --save-baseline writes project-wide dupes while audit --dupes-baseline scopes to --changed-since origin/main).

description: Fallow's `audit` is a heavyweight analyzer (multi-second wall-clock per CI run, requires coverage data, runs in v2.102.0 vs local v3.3.0 with different attribution — see meta-260712T1431Z-github-workflows-test-yml-pins-fallow-rs-fallow-sha-with-ve) that emits findings many of which a fast pre-flight lint pass could catch at a fraction of the cost. The current CI pipeline (.github/workflows/test.yml) jumps from `pnpm test` straight to `Fallow audit (PR gate)` with no syntactic/static-analysis gate in between, so trivial parse/lint/syntax errors consume the same wall-clock as structural dead-code or duplication findings. Add a linter (eslint + project rules, or a stripped subset of fallow's own rules) that runs in <1s and gates before fallow — shift simple-syntax findings to a cheap gate and reserve fallow for structural audits. Affects every PR's CI wall-clock and the agent's debug-loop cost when iterating on fallow findings that turned out to be syntax-level.

The agent runtime invokes fallow through MCP integrations but defaults to fallow's human-readable output, which is context-inefficient for LLM consumption. Per https://fallow.tools/docs/integrations/mcp/, MCP integrations should pass `--format json` and parse the structured verdict block (`verdict`, `attribution.dead_code_introduced`, `duplication_introduced`, `complexity_introduced`, `summary`) instead of running fallow and grepping the rendered report for `pass`/`fail` symbols. Human-readable fallow output (the verbose ✗/●/·/■ format) consumes several times more tokens than the equivalent JSON for the same findings, AND the runtime currently has to skim the entire output to extract a single decision. The runtime-side MCP integration should default to `--format json` and consume only the verdict + attribution fields. Verified empirically 2026-07-12: local `fallow audit --format json` returns the verdict + attribution block in ~3KB while the human-readable equivalent is ~30KB for the same diff.
resolution: Encoded as rule-fallow-brief-on-gate-failure (consult-checklist, 1 item). The originating finding's empirical claim about fallow output token cost was based on a 2026-07-12 measurement that does not match fallow 3.3.0 behavior on this codebase today. The right primitive is `--brief --format compact` — a one-line-per-finding CSV stream that replaces the gate's decorated human report. PROCESS_HINTS row #5 surfaces the hint at session start; cold-session parity test enforces mirror alignment. See plans/260714-1200-fallow-brief-discovery/reports/byte-size-measurements.md for Phase 1 measurement data.

description: The agent runtime executes `pnpm test` (and `pnpm test:cold-session`) then parses the human-readable node:test runner output by grepping for `✖` failure symbols across potentially hundreds of test names and assertion lines — this is context-expensive (each test name + assertion becomes token overhead) AND structurally wrong (re-running the full suite on each debug iteration re-floods the agent context with previously-seen passing tests). The test runner integration should expose a structured failure-summary endpoint (e.g., `node --test --test-reporter=json` piped through a small filter, or a custom `pnpm test:failures-only` script that prints only failing test ids + first-failure trace) so the agent can target its next action without re-reading passing tests. This is the symmetric problem to the fallow-MCP-format-json finding (meta-260712T1431Z-github-workflows-test-yml-pins-fallow-rs-fallow-sha-with-ve): both human-readable CLI outputs are inefficient when the consumer is an agent. Mitigation: a small wrapper script that runs the suite, captures failures, and emits a structured JSON `{ failing: [{ test, file, error }], total_pass, total_fail, duration_ms }` — the agent then iterates on failures only, never re-reading the passing-test backlog.
resolution:
Resolved by the Phase 2 atomic cutover (plans/260713-1625-vitest-migration-replace-node-test-c8, commit a6ab4a2 on plan/vitest-migration, follow-up 9b26d12 that tightened fallow baselines + R2 guard after the code-reviewer subagent flagged gaps). Two concrete improvements close the finding:

1. vitest's --reporter=json (configured in vitest.config.mjs) emits a single end-of-run document to .test-logs/vitest-results.json with numTotalTests + numFailedTests + testResults[].assertionResults[]. The agent (and the ck:cook workflow) can grep numFailedTests>0 and read only failing assertions. The structured-failure endpoint is the durable fix the finding asked for.

2. pnpm test is now 'vitest run && node tools/scripts/sanitize-coverage.mjs'. The hand-rolled 199-LoC namespaced runner (tools/scripts/run-pnpm-test-namespaced.mjs) is deleted. c8 is dropped from devDependencies. vitest 4.1.10 + @vitest/coverage-{v8,istanbul} added.

Phase 3 (post-cutover verification) is the registered follow-up; it owns the cold-session timing re-confirm, the 7 r2/ security test re-audit, the full fallow:gate sign-off, and the agent-context smoke test (grep .test-logs/vitest-results.json structured output without passing-test flood). Phase 4 (registry closeout) ships the loop-design.

description: Fallow CI gate passes via a 5-step recipe when 1+ findings block. Run on the SAME fallow major the CI pins (devDep `fallow` in package.json + SHA in .github/workflows/test.yml); mise-fallow and pnpm-fallow diverge on different majors, so debug with `which fallow` first to confirm which one CI runs. Step 1 — diagnose: `./node_modules/.bin/fallow audit --root <root> --gate new-only --changed-since origin/main --format json`; read `attribution.{dead_code_introduced,complexity_introduced,duplication_introduced}` and the `dead_code.unused_files`/`dead_code.unused_exports` arrays filtered to `introduced: true`. The verdict is `fail` iff any introduced counter > 0. Step 2 — classify each introduced finding: truly dead (zero references in repo) → DELETE; used via `#lib/*` package.json import alias → fallow can't follow aliases that resolve outside its `--root`, so suppress; used via `require()` from a `*.test.cjs` / `*.test.js` file → fallow ignores test files entirely so the requires are invisible, suppress; used via same-file throw/catch string-match (`throw new Foo(...)` + test `err.name === "Foo"`) → fallow only counts external consumers, suppress. Step 3 — apply suppression via tools/learning-loop-mastra/.fallowrc.json: for unused-files, add a glob to the top-level `dynamicallyLoaded` array (this marks the file as an entry point so its consumers are reachable); for unused-exports, add `{ "file": "<path>", "exports": ["<name>", ...] }` to `ignoreExports` (the schema is strict on properties — file + exports only, no `_comment` keys; see v3 schema at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fallow-rs/fallow/v3.3.0/schema.json `$defs.IgnoreExportRule`). The existing config has precedent for `core/meta-state.js` ignoring FILE_INDEX_FILENAME/getFileIndexPath/_resetFileIndexCacheForTests. Step 4 — regenerate baselines so the suppressed items are NOT carried as "accepted": `./node_modules/.bin/fallow dead-code --root <root> --save-baseline <root>/baselines/fallow/dead-code-baseline.json` (and same for `health` and `dupes`); saving with the suppressions active means future diffs that re-introduce these symbols will fail the gate, instead of being silently masked by stale baselines. Step 5 — verify: `pnpm fallow:gate` (or `pnpm test && pnpm fallow:gate`) must show `✓ No issues in N changed files`. Two v3-specific soft warnings are NOT gate-blocking and can be ignored: `WARN node_modules directory not found` (v3 looks at a different path on monorepo layouts) and `Warning: duplication baseline has N entries but matched 0 current clone groups` (v3 `--save-baseline` writes project-wide dupes while `audit --dupes-baseline` scopes to `--changed-since`). Pre-flight sanity checks: confirm `package.json devDependencies.fallow` and the `fallow-rs/fallow@<sha>` pin in test.yml are on the SAME major (gate-class divergence is exactly what this recipe was derived from); confirm `which fallow` reports the mise-fallow version the debug session uses.
resolution: Fallow CI gate now passes legitimately on v3.3.0. Steps applied in the unpushed commits that landed via PR #53 squash (c725b2c): (1) bumped fallow devDep + workflow SHA pin to v3.3.0 (14f8109); (2) suppressed 3 v3 false positives in tools/learning-loop-mastra/.fallowrc.json + re-baselined dead-code/health/dupes against v3.3.0 (3f41a67). Verified locally end-to-end: `pnpm test` -> 1840 pass / 0 fail; `pnpm fallow:gate` -> "✓ No issues in 46 changed files" audited against the PR base d4e9c8e (the exact diff CI scopes). mise-fallow and pnpm-fallow both report 3.3.0 (no major divergence). CI test check went green on PR #53 (test + registry-deltas + fallow all SUCCESS), PR merged. The 5-step recipe is proven and now codified in the suppressions/baselines, so future diffs that re-introduce these symbols will fail the gate instead of being masked. NOTE: the final merge blocker was a SEPARATE pnpm/action-setup@v6 self-installer lockfile crash (Cannot use 'in' operator to search for 'integrity' in undefined), not fallow; fixed by pinning pnpm/action-setup@v5.0.0 by SHA in .github/workflows/test.yml — outside this finding's scope.

title: Vitest migration — replace node:test runner and c8 coverage with vitest + @vitest/coverage-v8
description: Vitest migration (plan 260713-1625-vitest-migration-replace-node-test-c8) shipped via atomic cutover commits a6ab4a2 + 9b26d12 on branch plan/vitest-migration. Replaces hand-rolled 199-LoC per-namespace runner (node --test + c8) with vitest 4.1.10 + @vitest/coverage-{v8,istanbul}. Closes meta-260712T0730Z-test-runner-pollutes-agent-context structurally via vitest's --reporter=json structured-failure endpoint emitted to .test-logs/vitest-results.json (numTotalTests + numFailedTests + testResults[].assertionResults[]). The agent can grep numFailedTests>0 and read only failing assertions without re-reading passing tests. Loop-design-vitest-migration-replace-node-test-and-c8 is shipped (per change-log meta-260713T2032Z-test-runner-coverage). Path A (plans/260713-1503-test-runner-summary, the TAP-wrapper approach) remains marked cancelled.

CHANGE_LOG_BOUND_PATHS (tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/change-log-bound-paths.js:41-52) omits the runtime-surface hooks mirrors (.factory/hooks/**, .claude/hooks/**, .mastracode/hooks/**). The cold-session parity test enforces byte-for-byte equality between core/loop-introspect.js PROCESS_HINTS and .factory/hooks/loop-surface-inject.cjs LOCAL_PROCESS_HINTS (cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs:314), so .factory/hooks/loop-surface-inject.cjs is a real source-of-truth surface that gets edited. Yet Rec 12 gap-detection (isBoundPath) ignores drift on this path: editing the hook mirror without a change-log entry is not flagged as a gap. The detection set has skills mirrors for all three surfaces but no hooks mirrors for any of them. Scope decision needed: add only .factory/hooks/** (the surface actually in use) or all three runtime-surface hooks mirrors for symmetry with the skills entries. Note change-log-bound-paths.test.js:39-59 pins coverage via .includes() (not exact-equality), so adding entries will not break the existing test. Surfaced in plans/reports/journal-260714-fallow-brief-discovery.md followup (a).

The rule-entry pattern field is validated as z.string() with no shape constraint for consult-checklist rules (tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/meta-state.js:281-282 and the meta_state_promote_rule tool schema at tools/handlers/meta-state-promote-rule-tool.js:21). consult-checklist patterns are JSON blobs of shape {version, items:[{id, description}]} (see rule-fallow-brief-on-gate-failure in meta-state.jsonl). Consumers JSON.parse(rule.pattern) at runtime — e.g. gate-logic-consult-checklist-tool-integration.test.js:82 and gate-logic-consult-checklist-fallow-brief.test.js:55, and any agent runtime that reads loop_describe rules. Because the schema permits any string, a malformed pattern (e.g. "[not-json") would be accepted at promotion time and only throw at the first JSON.parse. Fix: add a zod refinement on pattern for pattern_type==="consult-checklist" that validates JSON.parse-ability and the {version, items[]} shape, applied in both core/meta-state.js metaStateRuleEntrySchema and the promote-rule tool input schema. Surfaced in plans/reports/journal-260714-fallow-brief-discovery.md followup (b).

No MCP tool exists to invalidate the running MCP server's ESM module cache. The server caches imports at startup, so edits to core/loop-introspect.js (PROCESS_HINTS) mid-session do not surface in loop_describe({tier: warm}).process_hints until the server restarts (journal-260714-fallow-brief-discovery.md lesson 4). Plan assertions about warm-tier live state are therefore confined to "process_hints count went up by 1" sanity checks, not full validation; the H6 ordering gate fires as a transient warning for any rule promoted before its PROCESS_HINTS row is visible to the running runtime. Proposal: expose a cache_invalidate MCP tool that re-imports (or clears the cache for) named core modules so future plans can refresh module caches without a server restart. Until it lands, the cold-session parity test (readFileSync-based) remains the source-of-truth validator. Surfaced in plans/reports/journal-260714-fallow-brief-discovery.md followup (d).

The byte-size measurements table in plans/260714-1200-fallow-brief-discovery/reports/byte-size-measurements.md could not capture non-zero-finding scenarios on this codebase because the current tree has 0 fallow findings vs origin/main, so only the clean-tree (~50 B) case was measured. The >=5-finding case (where fallow:brief's compact CSV stream is most valuable vs the gate's decorated human report) went unmeasured. Methodology fix for future measurement reports: include (1) a clean-tree baseline (e.g. `--max-crap 1` or current tree) AND (2) a synthesized-failure scenario — a temporary file with a deliberate high-complexity function — to exercise the >=5-finding case and record the byte delta between fallow:brief and fallow:gate output. Surfaced in plans/reports/journal-260714-fallow-brief-discovery.md followup (e).

The test-result-parsing procedure is not surfaced to the agent. The vitest migration (change-log meta-260713T2032Z-test-runner-coverage, vitest.config.mjs:54-57) shipped the structured JSON endpoint `.test-logs/vitest-results.json` (numTotalTests + numFailedTests + numFailedTestSuites + testResults[].assertionResults[] with status/name/message), but no PROCESS_HINTS row or discoverability hint tells the agent to consume it. So the agent re-pollutes its context by grepping raw vitest stdout and re-reading passing tests — re-introducing the anti-pattern of resolved finding meta-260712T0730Z-test-runner-pollutes-agent-context. That finding's resolution assumed "the agent can grep numFailedTests>0 and read only failing assertions," but the agent-facing instruction was never inserted, so every new session re-discovers the structured endpoint the hard way. Observed this session: the agent ran `npx vitest run <file> | tail -25` then hand-parsed `.test-logs/vitest-results.json` with a node script (reading numTotalTestSuites/numPassedTestSuites/numFailedTestSuites and filtering testResults for failingTests) instead of directly checking numFailedTests and iterating only failing assertions. Fix: insert a PROCESS_HINTS row in core/loop-introspect.js (mirrored byte-for-byte in .factory/hooks/loop-surface-inject.cjs LOCAL_PROCESS_HINTS, per the cold-session parity test cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs:314) describing the parse procedure — after `pnpm test`, read `.test-logs/vitest-results.json`; if numFailedTests>0, iterate testResults[].assertionResults[] where status=failed for {name, fullName, location, failureMessages}; do NOT grep raw vitest stdout or re-read passing tests.

**Fast-feedback gap (extension, observed 2026-07-14 16:56 BKK during fix of finding meta-260714T1630Z-after-the-mcp-server-restart-triggered-by-plan-260714-1358-r)**: The PROCEDURE row above covers parse-once when numFailedTests>0, but does not cover how to AVOID running the full suite when only one file changed. Observed in the same session: the fix ran the full `pnpm vitest run` three times (~25s each = 75s of test time) to surface pre-existing drift because no PROCESS_HINTS row instructed `vitest --bail` or `vitest --changed`. Same PROCESS_HINTS row should add three runtime flags: (a) `vitest run --bail=1` stops at first failure — use when iterating on a fix; (b) `vitest run <path>` runs only the affected test file — use when debugging one handler; (c) `vitest --changed` runs only files affected by `git diff` — use post-edit to verify the fix without re-running the 1883-test suite. These are runtime flags not parse procedure, but they belong in the same hint because they prevent the agent from re-running the full suite after every fix attempt. Also: this session's trial-and-error loop (3 vitest runs) is itself evidence of finding meta-260714T1656Z-meta-state-refresh-workflow-requires-n-round-trips-with-cache-mask — the meta-state refresh workflow gap that forced manual file-index refreshes between runs. So PROCESS_HINTS row update should land alongside the parse-procedure row in the same fix.

**Stale PROCESS_HINTS row**: Also refresh the stale PROCESS_HINTS row #1 (loop-introspect.js:123) which still references the deleted per-namespace `.test-logs/<ns>.log` log files from the removed 199-LoC namespaced runner — those logs no longer exist post-migration.
resolution: Shipped deterministic jq parse path: (1) tools/scripts/vitest-failures.sh — pure bash+jq parser (exit 0 green / 1 failed / 2 missing-or-invalid; truncates failureMessages to ~500 chars + …), covered by 7 hermetic vitest tests under tools/scripts/__tests__/vitest-failures.test.js plus a failed fixture at tools/scripts/__fixtures__/vitest-results-failed.json; (2) PROCESS_HINTS row #1 in tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/loop-introspect.js rewritten to surface fast-feedback run flags (vitest run --bail=1 / vitest run <path> / vitest --changed) + parse-once via the script + jq fallback + explicit Do-NOT hand-parse clause + retained same-file-read Rule 2, mirrored byte-for-byte to .factory/hooks/loop-surface-inject.cjs LOCAL_PROCESS_HINTS row #1 (cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs parity enforced); (3) loop-describe-warm-tier.test.js substring assertions updated to lock in the new contract (vitest-failures.sh + Do NOT instead of the removed silent-command Rule 1); (4) file-index re-grounded for tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/loop-introspect.js (2 findings re-grounded) since row #1 SHA changed. Acceptance: pnpm test green (1893 tests + 1 skipped); bash tools/scripts/vitest-failures.sh on green tree prints "all green: 1894 tests / 381 suites passed", exit 0; cold-session-discoverability 11/11; no new python-c/node-e parse of vitest-results.json introduced.

description:
The `projectHasLearningLoopMcp` predicate in `tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/gate-logic.js:534-543` checks for `.mcp.json` keys `"learning-loop-mcp"` or `"learning-loop-mastra"`, but this project's `.mcp.json` uses the key `"learning-loop"`. The predicate therefore returns `false`, silently filtering 3 active rules from `loadPromotedRules`:

1. `rule-project-skill-boundary` (gate-enforced glob, `pattern: ".factory/skills/{use-mcp,find-skills}/**"`)
2. `rule-import-chain-analysis-after-tool-deletion` (agent-checklist)
3. `rule-pr-body-registry-deltas` (agent-checklist)

All 3 have `scope_predicate: "project_has_learning_loop_mcp"`. The filter is applied at `core/gate-logic.js:600-606` BEFORE `applyPromotedRules` runs, so the 3 rules are dropped from gate enforcement entirely in this project.

**Empirical verification**: post-deploy of plan 260714-1358-rule-vocabulary-realignment (commit 83be7dfe14aeb98d90356ea4cb855d0e740f9f6f, 2026-07-14), `loop_describe({tier:"warm"})` returns `rule_count: 6`. The plan's §5.4b acceptance criterion claimed `rule_count === 9`, but that count did not account for this pre-existing filter. The 3 rules were already filtered before the rename — the rename did not cause the discrepancy, it just surfaced it.

**Semantic impact**: `rule-project-skill-boundary` cannot match `.factory/skills/{use-mcp,find-skills}/**` paths in this project because the rule never reaches `applyPromotedRules`. This is a correctness gap, not a crash — the gate silently no-ops for these 3 rules.

**Two remediation paths**:
- (a) Fix the predicate to match the actual key name (replace literals `"learning-loop-mcp"` with `"learning-loop"` at `core/gate-logic.js:539`). Smaller blast radius — predicate is code, `.mcp.json` is operator-facing config.
- (b) Fix `.mcp.json` to use one of the predicate's expected keys (`learning-loop-mcp` or `learning-loop-mastra`). Larger blast radius — affects operator-facing runtime wiring and may break downstream tooling that expects `"learning-loop"`.

Recommended: (a). The predicate's intent is "does the project have its own MCP server registered" — the key name is incidental; matching the actual server name is the correct fix.

**Verification command**: `node -e "import('./tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/gate-logic.js').then(m => console.log(m.projectHasLearningLoopMcp('.')))"` — currently returns `false`; should return `true` after the fix.

**Status**: pre-existing bug (not a regression from this commit). Surfaced during deploy verification of plan 260714-1358-rule-vocabulary-realignment.
resolution: Fixed by adding "learning-loop" as a third accepted literal at tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/gate-logic.js:539 (alongside existing "learning-loop-mcp" and "learning-loop-mastra" for backward-compat). Verified: predicate now returns true for project's .mcp.json. Added 2 new tests in gate-scope-predicate.test.js covering the canonical key and the legacy learning-loop-mastra key (closes the test-coverage gap that allowed the regression). All 99 tests in blast radius pass. No copy-pasted consumers of stale literals found in production source.

description:
After the MCP server restart triggered by plan 260714-1358-rule-vocabulary-realignment (commit 83be7dfe14aeb98d90356ea4cb855d0e740f9f6f, 2026-07-14), `loop_describe({tier:"warm"})` returns `"degraded": true` with the warning `"32 tool module imports failed; using manifest fallback"`.

**Observation**: The 32 tools listed in the warm tier response (gateCheckTool, gateOverrideTool, metaStateReportTool, metaStateResolveTool, ..., checkRuntimeAgnosticTool) match exactly the 32 tools in `tools/learning-loop-mastra/tools/manifest.json`. The warning suggests every handler module under `tools/learning-loop-mastra/tools/handlers/**/*.js` failed to dynamic-import during server startup.

**Despite the failure, tools work**: `loop_describe`, `meta_state_list`, `meta_state_refresh_file_index`, and `meta_state_report` all returned valid data during the verification flow. The manifest fallback provides tool metadata (name, description, schema) at startup; the actual `handler` functions must be loaded lazily on first invocation, where lazy-loading recovers from the startup failure.

**Risk surface**: 
- Cold-start tool calls may have a latency penalty (lazy-import on first use).
- If lazy-loading ALSO fails for a given tool, the fallback metadata hides the failure — the tool name appears in the manifest but invocation returns an opaque error.
- The degraded flag is set on the warm tier response but is not surfaced elsewhere (no `meta_state_report` finding auto-emitted for degraded startup).

**Root cause unknown**: was the manifest fallback active BEFORE the commit? Two hypotheses:
- (a) Pre-existing condition unrelated to the rename — the manifest fallback has been the steady state and the rename just happened to coincide with a verification call that surfaced it.
- (b) Regression caused by the rename — one of the 11 source files I edited (`tools/learning-loop-mastra/tools/handlers/meta-state-promote-rule-tool.js`, `meta-state-resolve-tool.js`, `loop-describe-tool.js`) may have introduced an import-resolution issue.

Hypothesis (a) is more likely because the rename was localized to enum literals + branches within handlers, not new imports. But this needs verification by restarting the server against `main` (pre-commit) and comparing the warm tier response.

**Recommended remediation**: 
1. Restart the MCP server against `main` and capture `loop_describe({tier:"warm"}).degraded` + warnings for comparison.
2. If pre-existing, file a separate remediation plan to investigate the import-resolution issue.
3. If a regression from the rename, identify which handler file's import is failing (likely the files I edited: `meta-state-promote-rule-tool.js`, `meta-state-resolve-tool.js`, `loop-describe-tool.js`).

**Not blocking**: deploy verification of plan 260714-1358-rule-vocabulary-realignment succeeded (registry state correct, PROCESS_HINTS loaded, H6 gate silent, rule-no-orphaned-evidence enforcement honored). The degraded flag is informational; the rename is functionally live.
resolution:
Root cause was NOT a regression from the rename — confirmed via git checkout 83be7df^ + rerun on clean main (same 32/32 failures, same `degraded: true`). The bug existed since commit 89fb6f2 (Phase E Plan 3 cutover) when `tools/handlers/` was created: `listAllTools` in core/loop-introspect.js never adopted the canonical "tools/X-tool.js" → "tools/handlers/X-tool.js" rewrite that server.js and build-meta-state-tools.js both apply. The rename just happened to be the verification call that surfaced it.

Fix shipped (Option B, centralize the rewrite): new facade `tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/manifest-loader.js` exporting `resolveToolFile()` + `resolveToolImportUrl()` with a TypeError guard that rejects non-`tools/`-prefixed inputs. Header documents the contract — direct inline rewrites are forbidden. Replaced inline rewrites at 3 call sites: core/loop-introspect.js:54, mastra/server.js:50, mastra/agents/build-meta-state-tools.js:45. Registered in core/placement.yaml per the Phase E invariant (manifest-loader.js is role=facade).

Test coverage: 3 unit tests in new `tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/manifest-loader.test.js` lock in the path shape, file:// URL form + actual dynamic-import succeeds, and the type guard. Tightened the existing degraded-flag test in `__tests__/legacy-mcp/loop-describe.test.js` from `typeof text.degraded === "boolean"` (which was locked-in to the bug) to `text.degraded === false` + assertion that at least one tool carries a non-empty `description` (proves the import succeeded). Two SP2/SP3 assertions updated from export names (`metaStateCheckGroundingTool`) to registered tool names (`meta_state_check_grounding`) — the warm tier's correct contract.

Verified: full suite 1883/1883 passes; `server-runid.test.js` logs "registered 33 tools"; `agent-direct-parity.test.js` instantiates intakeAgent with the correct tool surface. `listAllTools` now reports `{tool_count: 32, failures: 0, degraded: false}` (was `{failures: 32, degraded: true}`).

Side benefit (closing latent drift): 2 pre-existing drifts that were masked by the cold-tier cache surfaced once meta-state.jsonl's hash changed during the fix — gate-logic.js (drift since commit 83be7df, 3 findings regrounded) and manifest.json (drift since commit c725b2c, 1 finding regrounded). Both refreshed via `meta_state_refresh_file_index` with audit-trail reasons citing this finding.

Downstream work filed for the related UX gap: finding meta-260714T1704Z-the-meta-state-refresh-workflow-forces-n-trial-and-error-mcp + loop-design-meta-state-batch-refresh-and-reground-drift propose batch-refresh + auto-discover MCP tools + a 5-LOC cold-tier cache-key fix that would have surfaced both pre-existing drifts at commit time. Not blocking this resolution.

Live MCP server process still serves the stale response (degraded: true) until next restart — vitest in-process run proves the source change is correct.

description:
The meta-state refresh workflow forces N+ trial-and-error MCP round-trips + full-suite vitest runs to close file-edit drift, wasting operator time on every fix that touches code. Observed during fix of finding meta-260714T1630Z-after-the-mcp-server-restart-triggered-by-plan-260714-1358-r (manifest-loader centralization, 2026-07-14 16:30-16:53 BKK): (a) five sequential `meta_state_refresh_file_index` round-trips (~10s of pure MCP latency) for files I legitimately changed; (b) cold-tier-regression test failed three consecutive times against three DIFFERENT drifted paths — loop-introspect.js (from my fix), gate-logic.js (drift since commit 83be7df, ~7 days old), manifest.json (drift since commit c725b2c, ~12 days old) — each iteration cost ~25s of full vitest suite time = 75s of trial-and-error because pre-existing drifts were not discoverable up-front; (c) had to manually `rm records/meta/.cache/loop-describe-cold.json` between runs because the cold-tier cache is keyed on registry SHA only and masked the drift until meta-state.jsonl's hash changed.

Root cause is a 4-part API surface gap in core/loop-introspect-cache.js + meta_state_refresh_file_index + meta_state_query_drift: (1) refresh tool accepts one path at a time, no batch primitive — N edits require N round-trips; (2) no "auto-discover and refresh drifted" tool — operator must scan query_drift, copy paths, call refresh one by one; (3) cold-tier cache invalidation is keyed on registry SHA only, not on file-index.jsonl SHA — so file-index drift survives across cold-tier calls; (4) PROCESS_HINTS lacks a "refresh drifted files before re-running test suite after editing code" rule (the existing rule-no-orphaned-evidence gates resolve, not refresh).

This finding is the meta-state half of the same UX gap addressed in finding meta-260714T1334Z-the-test-result-parsing-procedure-is-not-surfaced-to-the-age (the vitest half: --bail, --changed, etc.). Both fixes should land in the same plan. Proposed loop-design: loop-design-meta-state-batch-refresh-and-reground-drift.

Recommended fixes:
- Tier 1 (one-file utility, ~40 LOC, 30 min): tools/scripts/refresh-drifted-files.cjs — walks meta-state findings, computes live SHA vs file-index.jsonl, calls meta_state_refresh_file_index once per drifted path. No MCP change. Operator-callable pre-commit hook.
- Tier 2 (batch MCP tool, ~50 LOC, 1 hour): meta_state_refresh_file_index_batch({paths: string[], reason: string}). Single round-trip replaces N individual refreshes. Backed by the existing refresh handler in a loop.
- Tier 3 (discovery+fix MCP tool, ~100 LOC, 1 hour): meta_state_reground_drift({scope: "all" | "commit:<sha>" | "session:<id>", reason: string}). Walks meta_state_query_drift output, bulk-refreshes every drifted path, returns {refreshed, still_drifted, drift_kinds}.
- Tier 4 (cache-key bug, ~5 LOC, 15 min): core/loop-introspect-cache.js invalidates the cold-tier cache on file-index.jsonl SHA change, not just registry SHA. Real bug, not just perf — pre-existing file-index drift survives across cold-tier runs because of this.

Impact: each fix that legitimately edits code currently burns 30-90s of unnecessary operator time on refresh round-trips + trial-and-error vitest runs. Across a typical 10-fix implementation plan that is ~10-15 minutes wasted per plan, every time. Severity is warning (workaround exists) not escalate (no data loss).
resolution: Closed by plan 260714-2012: Tier 4 (cold-tier cache-key fix on file-index.jsonl SHA, atomic paired reads to eliminate TOCTOU window) shipped in Phase 1; Tier 1 (pretest seed via reuse of committed seed-file-index.mjs with SKIP_PRESEED=1 escape hatch) shipped in Phase 2; PROCESS_HINTS row + 4-file mirror (incl. HINT_KEY_MAP_PROCESS coverage sibling test) closed the agent-discoverability gap in Phase 3. Tiers 2/3 (batch MCP tools) YAGNI per brainstorm §Batch MCP: pretest seed eliminates the operator mid-session pain those tools were scoped to address. meta_state_refresh_file_index keeps per-path audit semantics for deliberate individual regrounding.

title: Add batch-refresh + reground-drift to meta-state MCP surface; fix cold-tier cache key
description:
Add 4 additive changes to close the refresh-workflow gap surfaced by finding meta-260714T1704Z-the-meta-state-refresh-workflow-forces-n-trial-and-error-mcp (recorded 2026-07-14 during fix of finding meta-260714T1630Z-after-the-mcp-server-restart-triggered-by-plan-260714-1358-r). All changes are non-breaking — Tier 1 is a standalone script; Tiers 2-3 are new MCP tools (no schema drift); Tier 4 is a correctness fix to an existing cache key.

**1. tools/scripts/refresh-drifted-files.cjs (Tier 1, no MCP change, ~40 LOC, 30 min):**
Walks meta-state.jsonl findings with mechanism_check=true. For each cited evidence_code_ref, computes the live SHA-256 of the underlying file and compares against file-index.jsonl. For each drifted path, calls meta_state_refresh_file_index with the operator-supplied reason. Returns a one-line summary: `{refreshed: string[], skipped: string[], drifted_kinds: object}`. Reusable from pre-commit hook or post-edit cleanup. No new tool surface; the existing single-path refresh handler does the work.

**2. meta_state_refresh_file_index_batch({paths: string[], reason: string}) MCP tool (Tier 2, ~50 LOC, 1 hour):**
Single round-trip replaces N individual refreshes. Backed by the existing meta_state_refresh_file_index handler in a loop inside the MCP server (tools/learning-loop-mastra/mastra/server.js or a new handler module). Returns `{refreshed: string[], skipped: string[], audit_log: string[]}`. Idempotent on re-call (the existing handler is already idempotent). Must honor the same preflight + audit trail conventions as the single-path tool.

**3. meta_state_reground_drift({scope?: "all" | "commit:<sha>" | "session:<id>", reason: string}) MCP tool (Tier 3, ~100 LOC, 1 hour):**
Auto-discovers all drifted findings via meta_state_query_drift({run_grounding: true}), partitions by path, calls Tier 2 once with the union. Returns `{refreshed: string[], still_drifted: string[], drift_kinds: Record<string, number>, duration_ms: number}`. Default scope="all" matches the most common operator workflow (close all drift before testing). scope=commit uses git diff against the supplied SHA. scope=session is scoped to findings with session_id == <id>.

**4. core/loop-introspect-cache.js cache-key fix (Tier 4, ~5 LOC, 15 min):**
Currently the cold-tier cache is keyed on meta-state.jsonl SHA only. file-index.jsonl changes do not invalidate. This means pre-existing file-index drift survives across cold-tier calls — observed in this session as two pre-existing drifts (gate-logic.js since commit 83be7df, manifest.json since commit c725b2c) that masked the cold-tier regression test until meta-state.jsonl's hash changed for unrelated reasons. Fix: include file-index.jsonl SHA in the cache key alongside the registry SHA. ~5 LOC: import the file, hash it, include in the key.

**Test strategy:**
- Tier 1: tools/scripts/__tests__/refresh-drifted-files.test.cjs — fixture with 3 drifted paths, asserts script refreshes all 3 in one run; idempotency test (re-run, no double-refresh).
- Tier 2: tools/learning-loop-mastra/__tests__/legacy-mcp/meta-state-refresh-file-index-batch.test.cjs — call with N paths, assert N refresh events in the audit log; empty-paths guard.
- Tier 3: tools/learning-loop-mastra/__tests__/legacy-mcp/meta-state-reground-drift.test.cjs — pre-condition: 2 drifted findings; call reground_drift; assert drift count drops to 0 (via meta_state_query_drift after); commit-scope test with a synthetic drifted finding.
- Tier 4: tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/__tests__/loop-introspect-cache-file-index-key.test.cjs — pre-condition: drift in file-index but no registry change; call loop_describe cold; assert cache misses (was previously masking).

**PROCESS_HINTS row to land alongside (ties to the vitest-side fix in finding meta-260714T1334Z-the-test-result-parsing-procedure-is-not-surfaced-to-the-age):**
Add to core/loop-introspect.js PROCESS_HINTS (mirrored byte-for-byte in .factory/hooks/loop-surface-inject.cjs LOCAL_PROCESS_HINTS per the cold-session parity test): "Refresh drifted files before re-running test suite after editing code. After any Edit/Write that touches a finding's evidence_code_ref, run `pnpm refresh-drifted` (Tier 1) before re-running the suite. If `pnpm refresh-drifted` is unavailable, call meta_state_refresh_file_index for each changed path individually, then meta_state_reground_drift ({scope: 'all', reason: '...'}) when shipped. Skip step only when every changed file is *not* cited by any mechanism_check finding."

**Migration & shipping:**
All 4 tiers ship independently. Tier 4 is the smallest and most-correctness-impacting — ship first as a hotfix. Tier 1 is the next-fastest-win and unblocks operator workflows immediately. Tiers 2-3 require coordination with the MCP server manifest update (tools/learning-loop-mastra/tools/manifest.json) + the introspection handshake (see rule-runtime-agnostic-features for the 6-item checklist).

**Severity:** medium. Workaround exists (manual loop + the existing single-path refresh), but operators burn 30-90s per fix cycle. Not blocking adoption of any in-flight plan.

Pretest seed dirtying a tracked regen-artifact on every `pnpm test`/commit — git-workflow friction PARALLEL to meta-260709T1017Z-parallel-prs-that-each-commit-append-only-meta-state-jsonl-c. PR #58 prepended `node .../seed-file-index.mjs &&` to the `test` script (package.json:17) to absorb Edit/Write fingerprint drift at test time without manual `meta_state_refresh_file_index` round-trips. But `upsertFileIndexEntry` (tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/meta-state.js:679) always rewrites the ENTIRE `file-index.jsonl` with `updated_at: new Date().toISOString()` for EVERY entry, even when no `code_fingerprint` changed (JSDoc line 677: "updated_at is stamped with the current time"). Verified empirically 2026-07-15 during PR #58 review: running the seed with no code changes rewrites all 47 rows — the diff is PURE `updated_at` timestamp churn (every `code_fingerprint` is byte-identical in the `+`/`-` lines); `git status` shows `file-index.jsonl` modified. Consequences: (1) `git status` shows a dirty `file-index.jsonl` after every local `pnpm test` even with zero code changes; (2) the pre-commit hook (`pnpm test && pnpm fallow:gate`, package.json simple-git-hooks block) re-seeds AFTER `git add`, so the committed blob is pre-seed while the working tree is post-seed → dirty tracked file after every commit, and the committed file is immediately 'stale' relative to disk. This is SMALLER friction than the 30-90s N-trial-and-error refresh loop PR #58 removed, and the repo already absorbs `file-index.jsonl` churn via routine `chore(loop): refresh fingerprint index` commits (5 of the last 7 commits on this branch), so PR #58 shipped with the tradeoff ACCEPTED (non-blocking, COMMENT verdict from review-pr #58). Recorded here so the tradeoff is not silently lost. Root mitigations to debate NEXT SESSION alongside the parallel finding: (a) early-return no-op in `upsertFileIndexEntry` when `map.get(key) === hash` — eliminates no-change churn, but contradicts the documented always-stamp contract and changes `meta_state_refresh_file_index` no-op semantics (no longer refreshes `updated_at` on unchanged paths; `findings_regrounded` count semantics shift); (b) preserve prior `updated_at` for unchanged entries on partial rewrite so only the changed row's timestamp bumps — reduces churn on real edits but is not a true no-op; (c) gitignore `file-index.jsonl` and treat it as a pure build artifact regenerated at test/pre-commit time — removes the tracked-dirty-tree problem entirely but loses the committed baseline (cold-tier cache key, grounding coverage denominator) and parallels the M2 single-writer-gate debate meta-260708T0355Z-m2-single-writer-gate-commit-37a79e0-gitignore-47-49-the-rm. META-PATTERN uniting this finding with meta-260709T1017Z: BOTH are loop-GENERATED artifacts committed to git (`meta-state.jsonl` append-only change-logs, `file-index.jsonl` regen fingerprints) fighting against the loop's own regeneration — the unifying question for next session is whether loop-generated artifacts should be committed at all, or whether the committed-vs-regen split should be made explicit (some committed, some gitignored/build-only). Mechanism choice deferred to next session; this finding is the evidence argument, not the fix.
resolution: Fixed: true no-op in upsertFileIndexEntry (Phase 1) — re-upserting unchanged (key, hash) touches nothing on disk, keeps file-index.jsonl byte-stable so the cold-tier cache stays warm on no-change re-seeds. Plus commit/regen split (Phase 2) — file-index.jsonl is now an UNTRACKED regen artifact (gitignored; git rm --cached); CI seeds via env -u SKIP_PRESEED node .../seed-file-index.mjs --root=$GITHUB_WORKSPACE before cold-session probe tests (defense in depth + ensures SKIP_PRESEED inheritance cannot silently skip the seed). Verified: re-running seed twice produces no file-size or mtime delta (Phase 1 + Phase 2 together eliminate no-change churn).

description: CHANGE-LOG STREAM half of the superseded meta-260709T1017Z two-target mismatch. Parallel PRs that each commit change-log entries to meta-state.jsonl conflict at EOF on sequential merge — git cannot auto-merge same-position appends (observed 2026-07-09: PR #44/#45). Change-logs are IMMUTABLE (entry_kind=change-log, status=active forever; meta_state_log_change is append-only, no status flip, never mutated in place), so they are the SAFE candidate for a true-append-only file with the `merge=union` driver: union keeps both sides' distinct appended lines with no duplicate-id corruption. MITIGATION (Tier 1): (1) move change-logs to a separate change-log.jsonl; (2) add `change-log.jsonl merge=union` to .gitattributes, mirroring the shipped runtime-state.jsonl union entry; (3) extend the single read chokepoint — read-registry-cache.js hardcodes REGISTRY_FILENAME=meta-state.jsonl and every consumer (writes, relationship tools, schema validator) funnels through readRegistryWithCache — to also load change-log.jsonl so id-keyed relationship validation (meta_state_relationship_validate / meta_state_relationships / dangling_refs) sees the union and the file boundary stays invisible to id-keyed logic. NEW FAILURE MODE to handle: transient orphans (branch B change-log refs a finding on un-merged branch A) — resolve via a post-merge CI gate running meta_state_relationship_validate on the union (real orphans = typos/deleted-but-referenced; transient self-heal when both branches land). DESIGN NOTE for Tier-2 reuse: the read-chokepoint extension in MITIGATION step (3) must be shaped as a swappable projection seam (load sources → produce one entry array via a pluggable projection function), NOT a one-off two-file concat. Tier 2 will swap that projection to version-dedupe-by-id (max version wins) on the mutable file; building the seam once now avoids reworking the chokepoint a second time. Superseded parent: meta-260709T1017Z-parallel-prs-...; consolidation change-log: meta-260715T0630Z-meta-260709t1017z-...-append-only.
resolution: Shipped via PR #60 (merge f6766b3, 2026-07-15): change-log stream split into true-append change-log.jsonl with merge=union, dual-source read seam, 8-site immutability guard, consolidates z.array schema, migration, CI gates. Ship change-log meta-260715T1536Z-change-log-jsonl recorded this session. See docs/journals/journal-260715-1253-tier1-migration-shipped.md + journal-260715-1517-tier1-phase4-closeout.md.

description: FINDING STREAM half of the superseded meta-260709T1017Z two-target mismatch. The mutable entry kinds (findings, rules, loop-designs) in meta-state.jsonl are full-rewrite + in-place mutation: persistRegistryAtomic (meta-state.js:68-74) rewrites the whole file via tmp-rename; appendRegistryEntryAtomic (:76-83) reads-all-then-rewrites (NOT a true append); resolve/patch/batch/archive mutate entries in place. Therefore `merge=union` is UNSAFE on this file — union on a full-rewrite+mutation file keeps BOTH the stale base-version and the mutated version of a mutated id → duplicate ids → registry corruption (.gitattributes already documents this exclusion). This blocks the only code-free parallel-PR fix that preserves in-PR commits. Operator constraint: the hand-query `fx meta-state.jsonl '?.entry_kind != "change-log"' ... '?.status != "resolved"' ...` reads the RAW file as a TABLE (one line per id) — this is the operator's "what's next" workflow and depends on raw-file=table. A Tier-2 append-only + versioned last-wins dedupe rewrite would put N versioned lines per id in the raw file and BREAK this query (stale open-version lines surface as false-positive open findings); it would also force every direct reader (including the inbound gate's "last 20 raw lines") through a dedupe projection. CURRENT STATE: keep findings/rules/loop-designs as TABLE entries in meta-state.jsonl (operator fx unchanged). CURRENT SAFETY IS BEHAVIORAL, NOT MECHANICAL: the operator self-limits parallel finding-resolve because the mechanical fix (Tier 2) is absent — the table is NOT safely mergeable; the operator is the safety. The manual `git merge-file --union` workaround is a TEMPORARY BRIDGE pending Tier 2, not the resolution. TIER 2 IS THE COMMITTED NEXT PHASE after Tier 1 (change-log-stream split) lands — NOT deferred-until-symptom. The previous 'defer until a parallel PR actually MUTATES a finding' trigger is self-defeating: the operator self-limits to prevent exactly that event, so the trigger waits for a symptom that is suppressed → never fires → Tier 2 would defer forever → Tier 1 would decay into a workaround. Tier 1 is the staged prerequisite that narrows Tier 2's scope (change-logs offloaded, won't need version-dedupe); it does NOT touch the hard half (mutable table + routing every direct reader through a dedupe projection) and leaves the parallel-resolve speed limiter in place. Debt is paid when the mutable table is union-safe and the operator's self-limiter comes off. Superseded parent: meta-260709T1017Z-parallel-prs-...; consolidation change-log: meta-260715T0630Z-meta-260709t1017z-...-append-only.

Promoted regex rules in applyPromotedRules (gate-logic.js) were evaluated per-segment only: splitSegments splits a command on ; & | (honoring quotes), so no segment retained a literal pipe. A registry rule therefore could not match a cross-pipe pattern such as `vitest run ... 2>&1 | tail`, which made the State-3 backstop for raw-stdout parsing unexpressible as a promoted rule. Fixed by adding a full-command regex pass after the per-segment loop (safe strict superset: a pattern matching the full command either already matched a segment, or spans a removed delimiter — newly reachable; no active registry rule uses a literal `|`). The State-3 positive path is the `pnpm test:iter` wrapper; the promoted rule `rule-no-raw-stdout-vitest` is the deterministic backstop (operator to promote in live mode).

Agents repeatedly pipe `vitest run`/`pnpm test` stdout to `tail`/`grep` instead of parsing `.test-logs/vitest-results.json` via `tools/scripts/vitest-failures.sh`, across sessions 260715-1010, 260715-1100, and a014f8db. This is the canonical State-2 consumption limitation (per docs/philosophy.md): PROCESS_HINTS row #1 is deterministically injected but agenticly consumed, so adherence is non-deterministic — `--bail=1` (one salient flag) is remembered while the multi-clause parse discipline drifts. Promote to a gate-enforced rule (`rule-no-raw-stdout-vitest`) so consumption is deterministic (State-3): the bash gate blocks `vitest run`/`pnpm test` piped to `tail`/`grep`, and the `pnpm test:iter` wrapper is the positive path.

The canonical git union-merge driver command `git merge-file --union %O %A %B` is wrong — git merge-file writes its result to the first arg, so the result lands in %O (the ancestor copy), and git reads the unchanged %A (ours), silently dropping the other side. That is the exact data-loss merge=union exists to prevent. Corrected arg order: `git merge-file --union %A %O %B` (result lands in %A, ours, with %O as base and %B as other). Remediation: tools/scripts/setup-git-merge-drivers.sh sets the corrected driver per-clone, idempotent, with a wrong-order detection that refuses to silently overwrite (pass --force to acknowledge and overwrite). Documented in .gitattributes (comment) + AGENTS.md §8. .github/workflows/meta-state-refs-check.yml configures the driver on the ephemeral CI runner in a checkout step.

Branch-protection required-status-check satisfaction must be verified via the PR's mergeStateStatus (or commits/<sha>/check-runs[].name == required context), NOT via `gh pr view --json mergeable`. `mergeable` reports GitHub's merge-button state, which honors `enforce_admins:false` admin bypass — so an operator sees MERGEABLE regardless of whether required checks are actually satisfied, producing a false-positive completion claim. Concretely: a required check named after the Actions JOB id (`refs-check`) will never match a branch-protection context set to the workflow `name:` (`meta-state refs check`); the check stays PENDING while mergeable=MERGEABLE. Canonical mechanism: bind the required-check context to the parsed job id via `tools/scripts/setup-branch-protection.mjs` (single source of truth), and verify with `gh pr view <n> --json mergeStateStatus` == CLEAN. This finding reframes rule candidate `rule-required-status-checks-verify-combined-status` from the debug report at plans/reports/debug-cook-refs-check-context-mismatch-260715-1409-report.md.

Entry relationships (finding↔rule promotion, finding→change-log consolidation, rule↔rule supersedes, loop-design↔{rule,finding} addresses/proposed_design_for, finding→finding reopens) are implemented, validated, and retrieved across 4+ decentralized sites with dual-field + fallback logic, which misleads the agent runtime. (1) Schema: cross-ref fields declared per-kind in core/meta-state.js (promoted_to_rule at :317, plus consolidated_into, supersedes, addresses, proposed_design_for, reopens, origin). (2) Per-kind ref factories core/entry/{finding,rule,changelog,loop-design}.js each carry bespoke outboundRefs/inboundRefs; rule.js:68 documents 'Dual-field migration: rule.origin is the canonical promoted_to_rule ref' yet still scans finding.promoted_to_rule directly (rule.js:78) with a seenPromotedFrom dedup to avoid duplicate refs. (3) Inverse-index builder core/loop-introspect.js reconstructs promoted_to_rule_inverse from BOTH rule.origin (:616-623) AND finding.promoted_to_rule (:628). (4) Retrieval tools/handlers/meta-state-relationships-tool.js:182-190 applies a dual-field fallback, patching outbound promoted_to_rule from origin_inverse when the finding lacks the field. (5) Validation tools/handlers/meta-state-relationship-validate-tool.js only lints DESCRIPTION-string id refs (FINDING_ID_REGEX) — it does NOT validate structural cross-ref fields at write time, so a finding with consolidated_into=<dangling id> passes the lint. Symptom just observed: meta-state-promote-rule-tool.js:15 description still claims 'updates the source finding's promoted_to_rule to the rule id string' — stale vs the Phase-2 origin-canonical implementation (which writes rule.origin and does NOT touch finding.promoted_to_rule) — which misled an operator session into treating the intentionally-absent field as a missing inverse link. Recommendation: centralize into one source of truth for (a) which fields are cross-refs per kind, (b) forward + inverse resolution, (c) write-time structural referential-integrity validation (extend the validate tool beyond description strings to the declared cross-ref fields), and (d) the retrieval wire shape — so dual-field migrations don't leave vestigial schema fields, per-kind fallback patches, and stale tool descriptions that confuse the runtime.

Runtime context-injection is fragmented across overlapping surfaces with an undocumented division of labor and a duplicated, drift-prone source, which confuses the agent runtime about what is authoritative and where to get context. The surfaces: (1) SessionStart hook `.factory/hooks/loop-surface-inject.cjs` — push, cold, automatic at startup; per its header (:3) it claims to "inject loop_describe({tier:summary}) into context" but actually renders hardcoded LOCAL_DISCOVERABILITY_HINTS + LOCAL_PROCESS_HINTS copies plus a separately-spawned registry summary (the MCP server is spawned for counts but its hint strings are NOT trusted — security comment :14-16: "The server's discoverability_hints field is not trusted at render time. To update the hints, edit this file and commit"). (2) `loop_describe` MCP tool (loop-describe-tool.js) — pull, on-demand, tiered hot/warm/cold/summary; returns the CANONICAL buildDiscoverabilityHints()+buildProcessHints() (core/loop-introspect.js) PLUS dynamic `rules`/`tools`/`active_findings`/`loop_designs` read live from the registry. (3) `loop_get_instruction` MCP tool — pull, on-demand single-hint lookup by slug/index. (4) AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / `learning-loop` skill — static always-loaded steering prompt + prompt-author docs. Overlaps that confuse the runtime: (a) the SAME hints are delivered via two channels (push at SessionStart from a LOCAL copy, pull via loop_describe from the CANONICAL copy) with a parity test (cold-session-discoverability) enforcing they match — neither is "the" source, and editing one without the other drifts (this just caused a CI failure: rule promoted, canonical PROCESS_HINTS row added, LOCAL mirror missed). (b) Within loop_describe itself an agent-checklist rule appears in BOTH the dynamic `rules` field AND a hand-curated `process_hints` row; the H6 gate (loop-describe-tool.js:111-118) couples them by nagging "Add a hint referencing this rule to core/loop-introspect.js PROCESS_HINTS" rather than auto-generating — so one rule is represented in the registry entry, the dynamic rules field, a canonical hint row, a LOCAL hook hint row, and possibly AGENTS.md. (c) The stated principle (canonical/hook hint: "Each surface has a distinct role; do not duplicate content across them") is violated by design: the hook↔canonical hint duplication and the rule↔process_hints coupling ARE cross-surface duplication, kept because the hook deliberately mistrusts server hint strings at render time. (d) No documented division of labor between push (SessionStart) and pull (loop_describe / loop_get_instruction): an agent that already has the SessionStart hints does not know whether calling loop_describe warm tier adds anything (it does — dynamic rules/findings — but the hints block is the same content), and loop_get_instruction re-lookup of a hint already in the SessionStart injection is redundant. Recommendation: document the surface division of labor (push=cold-start fixed context, pull=current/deep context, on-demand=single re-fetch); make the trust boundary explicit and single-source the hints the hook DOES trust (e.g., the hook reads canonical via a node helper that imports the ESM core, or auto-generates process_hints rows for agent-checklist rules so promotion is one tool call not three); and reconcile the "do not duplicate across surfaces" principle with the hook's security-driven LOCAL copy (either trust the canonical at render with a signature/integrity check, or accept the duplication but make it mechanically enforced, not parity-tested). Related but distinct from meta-260715T2237Z-entry-relationships (that finding is about entry cross-ref storage/validation/retrieval; this is about the runtime context-INJECTION surfaces that deliver those entries to the agent).

Gratuitous mutations bump the CAS `version` counter and rewrite registry lines without any real field change, inflating the version history and obscuring real change history. Two worked examples observed this session. (1) TOOL-SIDE — `meta_state_promote_rule` unconditionally calls `updateEntry(root, id, { status: "open" })` on the source finding (meta-state-promote-rule-tool.js:190) even when the finding is ALREADY `open`. The promote tool's own comment (line 180-185) says "A promoted finding stays open" — i.e. the update is a semantic no-op — yet it still runs updateEntry, bumping version (0→1 here) and rewriting the line (git diff showed the branch-protection finding line replaced 1-del+1-ins with no field change other than version). Fix: guard with `if (entry.status !== "open")` before updateEntry, OR (preferred, DRY) make updateEntry short-circuit when the patch produces no field changes (no version bump, no rewrite) — one change covers the promote no-op and any future no-op patch. (2) AGENT-SIDE — I split a `meta_state_patch` into two calls (array-alone, then scalars-alone) to dodge the documented `{item:[...]}` wire-format wrap bug, which caused the loop-design version to jump 2→4 (two increments for one logical patch). But that bug is FIXED: the patch tool now derives its schema from the 4-kind record schemas (Bridge 5 — loop-design-schema-as-source-of-truth-bridge-5, shipped) and applies `z.preprocess(deepStripEnvelope, ...)` (the recursive envelope-unwrap, shipping the symptom fix from loop-design-meta-state-patch-wire-format-recursion); the tool description explicitly states arrays+scalars are envelope-stripped together "so the caller fixes the body instead of retrying wire shapes." So the array-alone/scalar-alone split is outdated ceremony — the stale guidance (the cross-reference-fields GAP note + the wire-format-recursion loop-design both still tell agents to split) outlived the fix. Fix: update the stale loop-design/finding guidance that prescribes the split, and note in the patch tool description that a single combined call is correct (it already implies this). Common root: no-op/defensive mutations that the system (tool-side) and the agent (side via stale workaround) both still perform, with no signal that they're gratuitous — the CAS counter and the git diff are the only witnesses. Recommendation: short-circuit updateEntry on a no-op patch (covers both), and prune the stale split-patch guidance.
resolution: Shipped via Plan 260716-1101 Tier 2 Phase B (true-append write path rewrite, 2026-07-16): updateEntry now has a canonical-comparator short-circuit (sorted-keys + set-semantics on arrays) that drops no-op patches before any file write. The promote_rule tool's unconditional updateEntry(root, id, {status:'open'}) call was replaced with a defense-in-depth pre-call guard `if (entry.status !== 'open')` so already-open findings no longer trigger a version bump. Resolves both worked examples in the finding: (1) the tool-side gratuitous mutation in meta-state-promote-rule-tool.js:190 and (2) any agent-side split-patch ceremony that the stale guidance might have produced. Change-log meta-260716T1403Z-tools-learning-loop-mastra-core-meta-state-js records the rewrite.

description: hasDrifted in core/stale-view.js is path-keyed-only: it returns true whenever the cited path is present in file-index.jsonl, without comparing the index baseline hash against the file's current hash. Because the pretest seed (seed-file-index.mjs) re-hashes every mechanism_check:true path to CURRENT bytes before each run, "path present in index" means "is a grounded path" — the opposite of drifted. The predicate's own docstring ("a present index entry means the file has been refreshed since last grounded") is false post-seed. Effect: isStaleView flags every grounded open finding as stale, so derivedStaleSet = all grounded open findings rather than a freshness signal. This contradicts the lifecycle-status-stale migration's thesis that "open is not stale, just deferred" and forced a regression-cap bump on every newly-filed finding. SP2 (check-grounding.js:206) already does the correct hash comparison (hashMatch = codeRefHash === storedFingerprint) and reports "grounded" for the same findings isStaleView calls stale — the two primitives contradict. Fix: make hasDrifted hash-aware (compare index baseline vs current file hash, caller-injected to preserve purity) OR have derived-stale consumers use SP2's checkGrounding drifted verdict instead of the path-presence predicate. This also makes meta_state_re_verify actually clear the drift signal — it currently cannot, since hasDrifted ignores timestamps and hash changes. Blast radius: derive-status.js (mechanism-shipped derivation), loop-introspect.js (cold-tier stale_view list), meta-state-sweep/relationships/relationship-validate tools, and stale-view.test.js which pins the current path-presence behavior.
resolution: Hash-aware hasDrifted shipped (plan 260716-0624). SP2-consistent predicate with TERMINAL_HASH_REGEX defense: drift = currentHash !== storedHash with index-then-per-record fallback chain. Phase 7 cold-tier drift-stale cap added as separate forcing function (zero in CI post-seed). meta_state_re_verify refresh is opt-in via refresh:true; CAS-ordering preserves consistency (no orphan baseline on conflict). Path-containment routing via resolveSafePath. Removes 0c8f670 workaround that scoped cold-tier cap to age-only.

description:
meta-state.jsonl carries duplicate id `rule-no-raw-stdout-vitest` (lines 12, 99). Line 12 (version 0, original pattern `(vitest run|pnpm test\b).*\| *(tail|grep)\b`) was appended-to on 2026-07-16 14:58 by commit d56f4bd0462 (fix(gate): PreToolUse deny-protocol); commit d56f4bd bypassed meta_state_patch and wrote line 99 (version 1, refined pattern adding `head`) as a brand-new row instead of patching the existing entry's content (per meta-state.js:472-474 refined_at/refined_by/refinement_reason primitive). The post-merge validator (validate-registry-refs.js:185-201 Plan 260715-1608 Phase 1 red-team F8) BLOCKs on any duplicate id across the union, which fires on every PR targeting main as soon as it sees the union of both files.

Hotfix paths via the canonical MCP surface are all blocked:

1. meta_state_patch cannot flip line 12 to status:inactive. The rule patch schema (meta-state.js:620, buildPatchSchemaFor case "rule": `metaStateRuleEntrySchema.omit({ entry_kind: true, status: true })`) explicitly omits `status`; no other field trick (id rename, version bump) breaks the count for `rule-no-raw-stdout-vitest` because line 12 still carries that id in the file.

2. meta_state_batch delete tombstones via Array.prototype.find first-match (deleteEntry: meta-state.js:1208-1249). find() returns line 12 (v=0), so the tombstone is built with version=1 (= currentVersion+1 = 0+1). After append, line 99 (v=1) and tombstone (v=1) tie at the same version. The gate's loadPromotedRules (gate-logic.js:570-636) uses the canonical projection (last-wins-by-max-version, tie-break on later created_at) and filters by status === "active". The tombstone wins the tie-break (later created_at ~ now vs line 99's promoted_at 2026-07-15T06:35:01.081Z) and carries status:archived, so loadPromotedRules EXCLUDES both line 99 and the tombstone, returning an empty set for that id. Gate stops blocking `tail|grep|head` — functional regression on the rule the original operator widened.

3. Direct Edit on meta-state.jsonl is blocked by the PreToolUse hook (matched_rule: "meta-state.jsonl") with the rationale "closes the parallel Write/Edit path identified in the audit-log gap investigation". No MCP primitive can physically remove a row from the file.

Resolution requires an out-of-band intervention: targeted revert of the meta-state.jsonl +1/-0 hunk in d56f4bd0462 (loses the registry dup but keeps the protocol-adapter fix intact), OR a gate_override + direct Edit (operator audit-trail captured, intentional bypass), OR an invasive validator + loader rewrite that survives the tombstone tie-break (out of scope for a hotfix; affects the canonical projection in core/meta-state.js:680-728, the gate loader in core/gate-logic.js:570-636, the validator in scripts/validate-registry-refs.js:185-201, and 1+ tests in __tests__/legacy-mcp/validate-registry-refs.test.js).

Blast radius: PR #65 (refs-check BLOCKED on main); every future PR that targets main until the dup is resolved; loadPromotedRules regression risk if the gate loader is touched without the validator patch; meta_state_batch delete by any operator on a multi-line-id entry will hit the same find()-vs-tie-break trap.
