resolution: Not registry corruption. Under Tier 2 Phase B versioned-append, the same-id/same-kind pair (rule-no-raw-stdout-vitest v0+v1) is the intended patched-rule representation, deduped by the read projection (core/meta-state.js last-wins-by-max-version); the gate loader (gate-logic.js loadPromotedRules) already loads v1 correctly and blocks tail/head/grep. The stale component was the post-merge validator's blanket duplicate-id guard (pre-Phase-B red-team F8), which false-positived on the legitimate versioned-append state and blocked every PR targeting main (PR #65 was the first casualty). Refined the guard (validate-registry-refs.js) to block only cross-kind id masking — the real F8 vector; same-kind multi-row ids are now legitimate. Regression tests added. See change-log meta-260716T1918Z. No gate override was needed: the fix PR is self-unblocking — its refs-check runs the fixed validator against the same-kind pair and exits 0. The finding's three 'blocked hotfix paths' were based on the pre-Phase-B premise that the duplicate row must be removed; Phase B append-only forbids line removal and the duplicate is the correct state.

The previous 'defer until a parallel PR actually MUTATES a finding' trigger is self-defeating: the operator self-limits to prevent exactly that event, so the trigger waits for a symptom that is suppressed → never fires → Tier 2 would defer forever → Tier 1 would decay into a workaround. Tier 1 is the staged prerequisite that narrows Tier 2's scope (change-logs offloaded, won't need version-dedupe); it does NOT touch the hard half (mutable table + routing every direct reader through a dedupe projection) and leaves the parallel-resolve speed limiter in place. Debt is paid when the mutable table is union-safe and the operator's self-limiter comes off. Superseded parent: meta-260709T1017Z-parallel-prs-...; consolidation change-log: meta-260715T0630Z-meta-260709t1017z-...-append-only.
resolution: Shipped via Plan 260716-1101 Tier 2 Phase C. Phase B rewrote the write path to versioned-append (tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/registry-append-atomic.js + appendRegistryEntryAtomic; no in-place mutation, no full-rewrite). Phase C flipped .gitattributes (meta-state.jsonl merge=union), added the Q2 same-id-concurrent-mutation CI advisory (one WARNING per affected id, pre-merge only), shipped the writer-side union-safety guard + per-clone driver CI check (both BLOCK), shipped the compaction signal (compact-registry.sh --check + --full, loop_describe warm-tier registry_stats), formalized the read recipe as `registry-table.sh | tail -20`, and proved end-to-end via a parallel-merge dry-run (two branches from a shared base each append a versioned line for the same id; merge=union keeps both lines; projection dedupes to last-wins-by-max-version). Operator self-limiter is removed: parallel finding-resolve PRs now auto-merge via union without producing duplicate ids or registry corruption.

description: Agents evade rule-no-raw-stdout-vitest by a two-command redirect-split (session cba9d506 lines 18-43, 81-85, 117-127): `vitest run <path> > /tmp/x.log 2>&1` (gate ok — splitSegments splits on ; & |, not >; the full-command pass sees no pipe) then `grep -E "Tests|FAIL" /tmp/x.log` (gate ok — no vitest token, no pipe). The gate is stateless and single-command, so no single-command regex can catch step 2. Root driver: no one-command focused runner existed, so agents invented a /tmp capture and grepped human vitest stdout. This reopens meta-260715T1328Z (same evasion lineage: tail->grep->head->redirect-split). Durable fix = incentive-removal, not regex refinement: add pnpm test:one <path> (tools/scripts/test-one.sh — vitest run --bail=1 "$@" 1>/dev/null; bash vitest-failures.sh) so the correct path is one command; teach vitest-failures.sh to sniff human stdout and point at test:one/test:iter; refresh the process_hints[0] one-file recipe. This keeps rule-no-raw-stdout-vitest as a speed-bump for the direct pipe while removing the reason to redirect at all.

The full-command second pass in applyPromotedRules (gate-logic.js:824-829) false-positives on investigative commands whose quoted grep/jq pattern contains the banned tokens. rule-no-raw-stdout-vitest pattern (vitest run|pnpm test\b).*\| *(tail|head|grep)\b matched across unrelated text when "pnpm test" and "| ... grep" both appeared inside a quoted search string alongside a real pipe elsewhere in the command (observed 2026-07-16T14:16 and 14:17 during this debug session: `grep -oE '"command":"[^"]*(vitest|pnpm test|...|grep)[^"]*"' <transcript>` and `jq -r '... | select(... | test("test")) ...'`). The left side of the alternation is satisfied by token text inside quotes, which should not count as a real vitest/pnpm-test invocation. Distinct from the redirect-split evasion (that is about cross-command state; this is about quoted-content leaking into the match). Suggested fix: strip quoted regions (single+double) before the full-command pass, mirroring the per-segment quote-awareness splitSegments already has, OR confine the left alternation to unquoted spans.

Agents repeatedly pipe `vitest run`/`pnpm test` stdout to `tail`/`grep` instead of parsing `.test-logs/vitest-results.json` via `tools/scripts/vitest-failures.sh`, across sessions 260715-1010, 260715-1100, and a014f8db. This is the canonical State-2 consumption limitation (per docs/philosophy.md): PROCESS_HINTS row #1 is deterministically injected but agenticly consumed, so adherence is non-deterministic — `--bail=1` (one salient flag) is remembered while the multi-clause parse discipline drifts. Promote to a gate-enforced rule (`rule-no-raw-stdout-vitest`) so consumption is deterministic (State-3): the bash gate blocks `vitest run`/`pnpm test` piped to `tail`/`grep`, and the `pnpm test:iter` wrapper is the positive path.
resolution: Resolved by incentive-removal rather than further regex refinement. Added pnpm test:one <path> (tools/scripts/test-one.sh: vitest run --bail=1 "$@" 1>/dev/null; bash vitest-failures.sh) as the one-command focused-runner, so there is no reason to redirect to a /tmp log; taught vitest-failures.sh to sniff human vitest stdout and point at test:one/test:iter; refreshed PROCESS_HINTS row #1 (canonical loop-introspect.js + .factory mirror, byte-equal). rule-no-raw-stdout-vitest stays as a speed-bump for the direct pipe. Full suite green (2128/432). Cascade from meta-260716T2220Z-…redirect-split which documents the evasion lineage.

description:
M2 single-writer gate (commit 37a79e0 + .gitignore:47-49 + the rm --cached in 171cd6e) groups 3 registry files — meta-state.jsonl, file-index.jsonl, runtime-state.jsonl — under one "uncommit from worktree" rule, but they have heterogeneous write profiles that were not debated per-file:
(1) meta-state.jsonl = operator-confirmed authority writes (MCP tools: meta_state_report/resolve/batch/log_change) + minor hook-append noise (MCP-connection-failure, recurrence). NOT auto-generated.
(2) file-index.jsonl = auto-regenerable derived artifact (refresh_file_index / seed-file-index recomputes it from the tree; plan 260704-0301 rule 3: "rebuilt post-merge, never merged").
(3) runtime-state.jsonl = runtime ledger (gate overrides, budget states, dispatch ledger rows) — session/runtime state.
Grouping them conflates deliberate authority writes with regenerable/runtime state. Two risks to debate carefully:
A. meta-state.jsonl arguably should be EXCLUDED from the gate (committable normally, not gitignored) since its writes are operator-confirmed, not auto-generated. Keeping it gitignored forces `git add -f` for deliberate migration commits and re-stale's the .gitignore once it is force-tracked (current state on branch lifecycle-status-stale-mechanism: meta-state.jsonl is tracked AND still listed in .gitignore).
B. Each uncommitted file must have a SINGLE consumer dependency; if it is consumed in multiple code paths, uncommitting it risks silently disabling those consumers. file-index.jsonl is consumed by grounding (checkGrounding/cold-tier-regression/query-drift/derive-status); runtime-state.jsonl by the constraint gate (readRuntimeObservations) + runtime_state_read + budget checks. All 3 files are tracked on main; merging this branch removes file-index.jsonl + runtime-state.jsonl from main's tree, with NO automated post-merge regeneration (no CI step runs seed-file-index.mjs; no hook). Verified: with the files absent, readFileIndex returns an empty Map and readRuntimeObservations fail-open returns [] — so main's CI stays GREEN but the loop's drift detection + constraint gate are silently disabled on fresh clones of main until a manual seed-file-index/refresh_file_index. This is a symmetric "remembered manual step with no automated guard" gap to the one M2 was built to fix — the gate closes the worktree-commit side but opens an equivalent gap on the main-post-merge side. Debate: (a) per-file gate rules instead of one grouped rule; (b) either automate post-merge file-index regeneration in CI (seed-file-index step) OR keep file-index.jsonl + runtime-state.jsonl tracked on main so consumers stay populated.
resolution:
Resolved by tier-2 migration PRs #64–#66 (commits 28400f7, a562512, 5a0ce4c, 1bf937c). Both risks closed:
- Risk A: meta-state.jsonl is now TRACKED on main, removed from .gitignore:47-49 (lines now list .claude/session-context.json, .claude/task-status-cache.json, docs/registry-summary.md). No more force-add + re-stale cycle.
- Risk B: file-index.jsonl is auto-regenerated by seed-file-index.mjs prepended to pnpm test (commit 28400f7); runtime-state.jsonl remains TRACKED with .gitattributes merge=union defense-in-depth. Drift detection + constraint gate no longer silently disabled on fresh clones.
Both debate alternatives implemented: (a) per-file handling replaces the M2 grouped gate; (b) auto-seed AND keep-tracked.

description:
The tier-2 versioned-append write path (PR #64 phase B) made meta-state.jsonl a multi-record-per-id file: an entry's v0 (open) and v1 (resolved) coexist on disk. The read chokepoint collapses them via `group_by(.id) | map(max_by(.version))[]`, surfacing the latest version. But `meta_state_list` (meta-state-list-tool.js:183-187) filters terminal statuses (resolved, superseded) AFTER the projection, with no first-class affordance to include them.

The only escape hatch is `include_archived: true`, which is misleadingly named — it surfaces all 4 terminal statuses (resolved, superseded, auto-resolved, archived), not just archived. This conflates two distinct concepts:
(1) "Show me archived entries" (a literal status filter)
(2) "Show me the full audit trail including terminal entries" (a separate post-collapse affordance)

Concrete impact observed 2026-07-17 in this session: after `meta_state_resolve` returned `{resolved: true}` for meta-260708T0355Z-..., verifying the resolve landed required `grep meta-state.jsonl | jq` because `meta_state_list({id: <id>, compact: false})` returned 0 entries — the terminal filter ran regardless of compact mode.

The versioned-append migration makes this WORSE because v1 (resolved) is now the canonical surface operators want to inspect (resolved_at, resolved_by, resolution text) for audit. Without a clean include-resolved affordance, the audit trail is gated behind direct file I/O.

Suggested fix: add `include_resolved: boolean = false` as a first-class flag distinct from `include_archived`, OR rename `include_archived` to `include_terminal` and document the precise scope. Optionally add `include_all_versions: boolean = false` to surface the versioned-append history per id.

The inter-finding relationship data model conflates three distinct mechanisms and the boundary between them is undocumented — causing a real conflation this session (two distinct findings share the meta-260716T2220Z timestamp; the `…redirect-split` finding's `reopens` edge was treated as a 'relationship' when the file-index was the right tool). Three mechanisms: (1a) 'findings related to a file' is already solved by `file-index.jsonl` (every finding with `evidence_code_ref` is grounded to its path; `check_grounding`/`refresh_file_index`/`query_drift` answer 'all findings on this file') — NOT a relationship edge; (1b) lifecycle lineage is carried by kind-pair-typed edges (`supersedes`, `consolidated_into`, `addresses`, `proposed_design_for`, `origin`) — keep; (2) 'solve one → resolve other' (cascade) is a closure POLICY (state transition) glued to the `reopens` edge via `cascade_from`, not a relationship type. Audit 2026-07-17: `reopens` set on 1/24 open findings (~4%, 1/102 total entries); the single parent `meta-260715T1328Z` was cascade-resolved (only observed cascade); the stale-status migration (plans 260611-1000, 260707-0812) already removed cascade's payoff — auto-closing a persisted-stale parent is no longer needed since 'open/deferred' is an acceptable resting state. Goals for the dedicated migration session: (a) STRENGTHEN (1a) — document the boundary explicitly in docs/meta-state-lifecycle.md + docs/architecture.md + a discoverability hint: file-index = 'findings on a file'; typed edges = lifecycle lineage; description prose = soft context; do NOT add a generic `related_to` field (DX trap: optional+vague → inconsistent → unqueryable); (b) MIGRATE (2) WITHOUT LOSING LINEAGE — drop `reopens` + `cascade_from` + `reopens_inverse` (core/loop-introspect.js:556,580,588,642-647,751) + the stale-guard in `meta_state_resolve`; on stale-succession, open a fresh finding and carry lineage as free-text in `description` (greppable, zero schema, zero orphan-ref risk); resolve parents by hand; add a closure-directive (`cascade_resolve:true`) as a SEPARATE concept layered on a relationship ONLY if a real recurrence cluster (>2 linked findings) appears (YAGNI). Migration safety (rule hint 7): import-chain analysis, not keyword-grep, before deleting; preserve ref-validation + canonical-compare. description: meta_state_patch returns {patched: true, version: N} on an empty patch object — observed 2026-07-17 during debug session when calling the tool twice with `patch: {}` to update consolidated_into on a superseded finding. The empty patch landed both times with patched:true despite no actual mutation. Root cause spans three permissive layers + one overloaded short-circuit: (1) `metaStateEntryPatchSchema = z.object({}).passthrough()` at core/meta-state.js:633 accepts any shape including {}; (2) per-kind `.partial().strict()` at core/meta-state.js:618 makes every field optional; (3) meta-state-patch-tool.js handler has no empty-check before calling updateEntry; (4) updateEntry's no-op short-circuit at core/meta-state.js:1128 (`if (entriesEqual(patched, existingEntry)) return true`) was added for legitimate no-ops (resolves finding meta-260715T2311Z-gratuitous-mutations) but overloads the patched:true response — caller cannot distinguish "wrote a new line" from "nothing changed". Concrete cost: the operator's CLI feedback loop loses signal — a typo'd patch path or missing field silently succeeds, masking the bug. Defense-in-depth fix: (a) schema refine at line 633 to reject empty objects; (b) handler-level empty-check after immutable-fields deny-list with reason `empty_patch` and a hint pointing at the right tool for status/consolidated_into/resolved_at operations (meta_state_supersede / meta_state_resolve). Out of scope: meta_state_batch update op has the same exposure via .passthrough() at line 23 — schema refine (a) catches it for direct core callers; the handler check (b) is patch-specific. Reproducer: report a finding, then call meta_state_patch({id, entry_kind:'finding', patch:{}, _expected_version:0}).

resolution: Superseded by change-log meta-260717T1034Z. Defense-in-depth fix: (1) core/meta-state.js:633 metaStateEntryPatchSchema now .refine()s against empty objects at the schema boundary (catches direct core callers); (2) meta-state-patch-tool.js adds an explicit empty-patch check after the immutable-fields deny-list that returns {patched:false, reason:"empty_patch", hint:"...meta_state_supersede | meta_state_resolve | meta_state_log_change"} before the CAS field is added (so the MCP-wire path sees the rejection for the patch the user actually sent). Public contract change: empty patches now return {patched:false, reason:"empty_patch"} instead of {patched:true, version:<unchanged>}. Legitimate no-ops via entriesEqual (e.g., setting category to its current value) are still short-circuited correctly with no version bump. Two new tests cover the user-facing paths; two pre-existing tests in meta-state-patch-entry-kind-invariant.test.js were updated to assert the new contract (the registry-state invariant they were guarding is preserved — load-bearing assertion is registry state, not return value). Verification: full suite 2148 tests / 436 suites green after seed step prepended. Side-effect sweep: meta_state_batch update op has the same schema exposure via .passthrough() at batch-tool.js:23 (the schema refine catches it for direct core callers; no parallel handler check added — separate fix). Out of scope.

description: The plan-260704-0301 decision to keep dispatch and ack orthogonal (plan.md:144 — "Dispatch is built compatible (explicit resolve, no ack, delegated_to recorded in the ledger)") was correct v1, given that the lifecycle-redesign plan (drop ack/active, collapse to one open state, re-architect OPERATOR_MODE → delegated scoped authority) was deferred. The dispatch feature shipped 2026-07-04 (PR #33) and is now in live use, producing immediate evidence that the deferred lifecycle decision is no longer safely deferrable. Observed in this session: meta-260704T0933Z-local-fallow-gate-cannot-fully-self-verify-and-the-file-inde dispatched to issue #34 in dat9uy/learning-loop-template (commit landed in operator window; ledger_ref patched, runtime-state dispatch row written, issue OPEN); registry entry still shows status=reported with expires_at=2026-07-05T02:33:01.794Z. The TTL clock will age this finding to stale, surface it again in Rec 10 dispatch hints, or auto-resolve — none of which reflect operator intent (the operator already acted by creating the issue + committing the ledger). The dispatch tool itself is correct-by-plan; the inconsistency is between the plan (no ack on dispatch) and operator intent (a dispatched finding is a deferred decision), surfaced by real use on day 1 of production. This is not a fix to apply — it is an evidence artifact that argues for prioritizing the lifecycle-redesign brainstorm over its current deferred slot. The brainstorm must resolve three coupled questions: (1) keep status=active (with explicit ack) and have dispatch drive it; (2) collapse to one open state and have dispatch be a metadata flag instead of a status transition; (3) introduce a third state (e.g., deferred) distinct from both reported and active. Until then, dispatch behavior is correct but inconsistent-with-operator-intent — a documented tension to brainstorm, not a code change to ship.
resolution: Ack mechanism removed by plan 260707-0812; lifecycle collapsed to single 'open' state. The brainstorm's option (2) — dispatch as metadata flag (ledger_ref, no status transition) — won by migration. Options (1) ack-driven 'active' and (3) 'deferred' state never built. Consolidated into meta-260717T1057Z.

description: No automated registry-consistency check exists to detect entries whose `status` and audit fields disagree. After the manual restore in session 06085a38, meta-state.jsonl:19 carried `status: active` together with `resolved_at` + `resolution` (Finding 4 of plans/reports/debugger-260614-0207-session-06085a38-meta-state-process-gaps.md). The P0 cleanup removed the visible residue, but a defense-in-depth probe is needed so the next drift is caught at the registry layer, not by an operator. Expected invariants: `status: active` must not carry `resolved_at` / `resolution`; `status: archived` must carry `archived_at` / `archived_by` / `archived_reason`; `status: resolved` must carry `resolved_by`; `status: superseded` must carry `consolidated_into`. Remediation: add a test under tools/learning-loop-mcp/__tests__/ that walks the live registry and fails on any status/audit-field mismatch, OR expose a `meta_state_consistency_check` MCP probe that returns the list of drifted entries without mutating state.
resolution: Remediated. meta_state_consistency_check MCP probe shipped (tools/learning-loop-mastra/tools/handlers/meta-state-consistency-check-tool.js) with pure invariant detector (core/consistency-check.js, META_STATE_CONSISTENCY_INVARIANTS). Tests at both layers (core/__tests__/consistency-check.test.js, __tests__/legacy-mcp/meta-state-consistency-check-tool.test.js). Registered in manifest.json:33. Plan: plans/260626-1734-phase-e-registry-drift-fix. Follow-up: F-1 invariant references deleted 'status: active' — separate finding filed for invariant-set pruning.

description: The consistency-check invariant set in core/consistency-check.js (META_STATE_CONSISTENCY_INVARIANTS) was authored under the pre-migration status enum (active/reported/resolved/superseded/archived/expired/auto-resolved). Plan 260707-0812 deleted status:active, status:reported, status:auto-resolved, status:expired; the canonical post-migration enum is now {open, resolved, superseded, archived}. The F-1 invariant row references the deleted status='active' — so the pure detector encodes an invariant that cannot fire on any live entry. The result is a probe that ships with silently-drifted invariants: F-2/F-3/F-4 still apply, NEW-1 (status='reported') is also dead. Remediation: audit each row against the new canonical enum, prune rows whose status is deleted, and re-anchor any constraint that needs to survive — e.g., the F-1 spirit ('open must not carry terminal audit fields') on status='open' with the forbid list drawn from the post-migration writeEntry contract. Source-of-truth for what audit fields 'open' can legitimately carry is the write-path invariants at tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/meta-state.js (resolve/supersede/archive handlers).

The new stripNodeEvalBody function in tools/learning-loop-mcp/core/gate-logic.js blanks the body of node -e|--eval|-p|--print wrappers before constraint-pattern regex matching. Trade-off: node -e "require('child_process').exec('npm install')" no longer matches the package-manager constraint (the command is inside the blanked body). This is an accepted bypass, not a fix; the user-stated design from plans/reports/brainstorm-260615-1300-bash-gate-debate-friendly-and-string-literal-fix.md#plan-2 chose asymmetry (only node, not python-c/bash-c) because python -c "import docker" IS a real command and the body must remain visible (existing tests at __tests__/gate-logic-quoted-strings.test.js lock this in), and the node -e pattern is rare in real agent flows. Catch-net: the gate_check_recurrence MCP tool shipped in plan 260615-1530-bash-gate-debate-stderr-override-recurrence auto-files a finding via meta_state_report if node -e "..." matches a constraint N>=3 times in M<=10min. The recurrence tracker reads .gate-decision.log and groups by rule_id + command_prefix_normalized. The operator/agent can resolve this finding manually by refining the strip or by promoting a new gate rule that catches node -e wrappers specifically.

description:
`meta_state_re_verify` is the lone operator-tier meta-state tool still using the inline `META_STATE_VERIFY_EXEC=1` env-var gate at `meta-state-re-verify-tool.js:29` — 5 sibling tools (`meta_state_supersede`, `meta_state_promote_rule`, `meta_state_ship_loop_design`, `meta_state_dispatch_finding`, `runtime_state_record`) have already migrated to `LOOP_SESSION_MODE=live` via `tools/lib/session-mode.js#isLiveSession`. The original 3-layer defense-in-depth design from `plans/260609-stale-flag-redesign/plan.md:142,206,218` locked in `(1) META_STATE_VERIFY_EXEC=1 env-gate; (2) cmd-allowlist from core/patterns.json#meta-state-verify-cmd-allowlist; (3) spawnSync with shell:false + 10s timeout`. The 260707 rework (`plans/260707-0812-.../phase-03:46,55`) re-affirmed: "`META_STATE_VERIFY_EXEC` gate preserved." Two questions sit in the gap between "preserved" and "migrated to canonical helper pattern":

(A) STAY SEPARATE from `LOOP_SESSION_MODE=live`. Verify is the only operator-tier tool that runs external processes — its threat surface (shell injection from registry-stored commands) is qualitatively different from "mutates registry" tools. Merging collapses Layer 1 (the process-level kill-switch), breaks CI/test isolation (`OPERATOR_MODE=1` is set for fixture writes without enabling exec), and creates a foot-gun. The defense-in-depth is load-bearing, not vestigial.

(B) MIGRATE to the canonical `#lib/*-mode.js` helper pattern. Extract `tools/lib/exec-mode.js` mirroring `session-mode.js` (exports `isExecSession()` returning `process.env.META_STATE_VERIFY_EXEC === "1"`); refactor `meta-state-re-verify-tool.js:29` to call it. Extend `session-mode.js` JSDoc with a sibling-pointer: "session-mode authorizes operator-tier mutations; exec-mode authorizes running registry-stored commands." Fills red-team gap C3 from `plans/260626-0607-phase-e-housekeeping/reports/general-purpose-260626-0616-...` ("Plan does not document how to set `META_STATE_VERIFY_EXEC=1` in the MCP server's environment").

Resolution path: (i) ship `tools/lib/exec-mode.js#isExecSession` (zero behavior change); (ii) refactor `meta-state-re-verify-tool.js:29` to call it; (iii) extend `session-mode.js` JSDoc with sibling-pointer; (iv) decision on `.mcp.json`: add `META_STATE_VERIFY_EXEC=1` env if operator wants re_verify to actually run registry-stored steps in this session, or leave unset + use operator-audited `meta_state_patch` direct stamp (precedent: `plans/260626-0607-phase-e-housekeeping/phase-05-i2-re-verify-entry-9.md:201-204`). Acceptance: re_verify behavior unchanged; implementation surface aligns with sibling tools; `.mcp.json` env field becomes documented.

Operational symptom: in this Claude Code session `meta_state_re_verify` returns `verify_exec_required` because `.mcp.json` boots with only `LOOP_SURFACE=.claude`; `META_STATE_VERIFY_EXEC` is unset. Operator-audited patch path (via `meta_state_patch` direct `last_verified_at` write) is the documented workaround for entries without `verification.steps`. Compounds with `meta-260717T1101Z-the-consistency-check-invariant-set-in-core-consistency-chec` — both surface meta-state tool implementations drifting from canonical helper patterns; together they justify a "tool-grammar-consistency" audit (possible rule-promotion candidate).

Plan 260717-1145 root cause: meta_state_patch and meta_state_batch update op silently no-op on empty input — the model-visible schema (union of 4 .partial().strict() branches at core/meta-state.js:586/616-621) makes `{}` the unique safe emission because Zod .refine is dropped by z.toJSONSchema (verified empirically: .refine on z.object({a:z.string()}) emits no refine/minLength in draft-7). Runtime rejection does not steer constrained decoding: session e10944c4 emitted `patch:{}` five consecutive times after commit 6ee3186 shipped the runtime empty_patch rejection. The prior finding meta-260717T1026Z captured only the silent-success SYMPTOM; this finding captures the CAUSE (schema shape making {} the zero-risk emission) and the layered fix. Fix deployed in plan 260717-1145: (Phase 2) parity-seam patch.minProperties:1 injection on the model-visible JSON schema via create-loop-tool.js#attachParityJSONSchema — generation-only, .parse() unchanged; (Phase 3) per-branch validation against buildPatchSchemaFor(entry_kind) in meta_state_patch handler + per-update-op validation against the existing entry's kind in meta_state_batch handler — runtime floor with localized field_errors; (Phase 4) schema-derived content-aware empty_patch / no_content hints naming the per-kind mutable fields (description, evidence_code_ref first). Sibling fix: meta_state_batch update op merges content inline via .passthrough() at meta-state-batch-tool.js:23 — different exposure shape (no nested patch:{}, discriminator op:\"update\" already prevents bare {}), so the lever is runtime only: zero-content updates return reason:\"no_content\" + bad inline fields return reason:\"invalid_field\" with field_errors. Defense-in-depth preserved: metaStateEntryPatchSchema.refine at core/meta-state.js:641 + the empty_patch runtime check at meta-state-patch-tool.js stay as safety nets. Open hypothesis (out of scope for code): whether the Claude Code harness feeds tool schema-validation errors back to the model as retryable steering — Phase 2 alone breaks the loop iff yes, else Phases 3+4 are the guaranteed floor; plan ships both layers regardless. Reopens meta-260717T1026Z-meta-state-patch-returns-patched-true-version-n-on-an-empty (the prior finding is already status:superseded by a change-log, so reopens flags the unresolved-cause lineage).

The tier-2 versioned-append write path (PR #64 phase B) made meta-state.jsonl a multi-record-per-id file: an entry's v0 (open) and v1 (resolved) coexist on disk. The read chokepoint collapses them via `group_by(.id) | map(max_by(.version))[]`, surfacing the latest version. But `meta_state_list` (meta-state-list-tool.js:183-187) filters terminal statuses (resolved, superseded) AFTER the projection, with no first-class affordance to include them.

The only escape hatch is `include_archived: true`, which is misleadingly named — it surfaces all 4 terminal statuses (resolved, superseded, auto-resolved, archived), not just archived. This conflates two distinct concepts:
(1) "Show me archived entries" (a literal status filter)
(2) "Show me the full audit trail including terminal entries" (a separate post-collapse affordance)

Concrete impact observed 2026-07-17 in this session: after `meta_state_resolve` returned `{resolved: true}` for meta-260708T0355Z-..., verifying the resolve landed required `grep meta-state.jsonl | jq` because `meta_state_list({id: <id>, compact: false})` returned 0 entries — the terminal filter ran regardless of compact mode.

The versioned-append migration makes this WORSE because v1 (resolved) is now the canonical surface operators want to inspect (resolved_at, resolved_by, resolution text) for audit. Without a clean include-resolved affordance, the audit trail is gated behind direct file I/O.

Suggested fix: add `include_resolved: boolean = false` as a first-class flag distinct from `include_archived`, OR rename `include_archived` to `include_terminal` and document the precise scope. Optionally add `include_all_versions: boolean = false` to surface the versioned-append history per id.
resolution: Plan 260717-1451-meta-state-list-include-all-versions shipped the include_all_versions flag on meta_state_list (Phases 1-3). The MCP tool now surfaces the versioned-append history per id, bypassing the max_by(version) projection via the new readRegistryAllVersions core helper (cache value shape {projected, allVersions} in core/read-registry-cache.js; shared readRawLines helper in core/meta-state.js). The flag is orthogonal to include_archived (status filter) and compact (projection shape); the three compose. Shell-side symmetric flag: tools/scripts/registry-table.sh --all-versions. Discoverability: tool description + AGENTS.md §6.1 audit-trail recipe + CLAUDE.md quick-reference. Operators no longer need grep+jq to inspect versioned-append history.

pattern: {"version":1,"items":[{"id":"same-commit-dependency","description":"When a workflow adds pnpm exec <tool>, npx <tool>, or npm run <script>, the tool MUST be in devDependencies (or dependencies) in the SAME commit. Verify with `grep '<tool>' package.json` after any .github/workflows/*.yml edit. Symptom of skip: CI's `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` fails with `command not found` on the first PR."},{"id":"baseline-flag-format","description":"When wiring `fallow audit` in CI, generate baselines with `fallow <sub> --save-baseline <path>` (audit format: array of `path:export` strings). NEVER `--save-regression-baseline` (regression format: nested objects). The two flags produce INCOMPATIBLE JSON; the audit --*-baseline flag fails to parse the regression format."},{"id":"baseline-storage","description":"`fallow` auto-creates `<root>/.fallow/.gitignore: *` that silently gitignores `<root>/.fallow/baselines/`. Verify `git ls-files <root>/.fallow/baselines/` returns expected files BEFORE committing. Prefer `<root>/baselines/fallow/` (committed, outside the auto-ignored cache); the previous `plans/<plan-slug>/reports/fallow/` location is deprecated because it couples CI to a plans directory. Baselines are package-scoped: each fallow-audited package gets its own `<root>/baselines/fallow/`."},{"id":"third-party-action-sha-pin","description":"When swapping a hand-rolled `pnpm exec <tool>` / `npx <tool>` for a third-party GitHub Action (`uses: <vendor>/<tool>@<ref>`), pin to commit SHA, not tag (`uses: <vendor>/<tool>@<sha>`). Verify the Action provides cryptographic signature verification (e.g., fallow-rs/fallow v2 uses Ed25519 + SHA-256 + sentinel at npm/fallow/scripts/{verify-binary,lazy-verify,run-binary}.js) and assert `verified: yes (<sentinel-path>)` in the CI logs. Tag-based pins drift silently when the upstream re-tags; SHA pins survive tag deletion. CLI binary version is pinned separately via `version:` input (or read from `package.json devDependencies.<tool>` via a setup step)."}]}
description: Tool integration hygiene: same-commit dependency, baseline flag format, baseline storage (in-package, not plans/), and 3rd-party Action SHA pin + crypto verify.
hint_text: Tool integration checklist. Before wiring a new tool into CI or repo automation, consult the 4-item checklist in `rule-tool-integration-same-commit-dep`: (1) same-commit dependency — if the workflow adds `pnpm exec <tool>` / `npx <tool>` / `npm run <script>`, the tool MUST be in `devDependencies` in the SAME commit; (2) baseline flag format — `fallow <sub> --save-baseline` (audit) and `--save-regression-baseline` (regression) produce INCOMPATIBLE JSON; (3) baseline storage — `fallow` auto-creates `.fallow/.gitignore: *`; use `<root>/baselines/fallow/` (NOT `plans/<slug>/reports/fallow/`, which couples CI to a plans directory); (4) third-party Action SHA pin — when swapping `pnpm exec <tool>` for `uses: <vendor>/<tool>@<commit-sha>`, pin to commit SHA (not tag) and verify the Action provides cryptographic signature verification (e.g., fallow-rs/fallow v2: Ed25519 + SHA-256 + sentinel at `npm/fallow/scripts/{verify-binary,lazy-verify,run-binary}.js`); `fallow-rs/fallow@<commit-sha>` is the canonical example. See `tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/README.md` §Tool integration checklist.

hint_text: Fallow gate triage. When `pnpm fallow:gate` (or any local `fallow audit --gate new-only`) exits non-zero from pre-commit, do NOT re-parse the human-readable prose. Run `pnpm fallow:brief` next: it emits a compact-CSV stream (one finding per line: `high-complexity:<path>:<line>:<symbol>:cyclomatic=N,severity=<level>,crap=N,...`). The brief stream is much smaller than the gate's decorated human report and is machine-actionable when at least one finding exists — grep for `severity=` (filter by the finding's actual severity per its meta-state entry, which may be `warning` not `high`); ignore baseline-inherited lines. On a clean tree the brief is ~50 B with no action needed. See `rule-fallow-brief-on-gate-failure` in `meta-state.jsonl` for the full contract.

pattern:
Before claiming a GitHub branch-protection required status check is satisfied:
  1. Read the branch-protection required_status_checks via `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/branches/<branch>/protection/required_status_checks` and note the required `context` string(s).
  2. Read the commit's check runs via `gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/commits/<sha>/check-runs` and confirm a check run whose `name` equals a required `context` exists with `conclusion: "success"`. (GitHub matches required checks against the check-run NAME, which for Actions = the JOB id, NOT the workflow `name:` field.)
  3. Confirm satisfaction via `gh pr view <n> --json mergeStateStatus` == "CLEAN" (or the combined commit status), NOT via `gh pr view --json mergeable`. `mergeable` reports the merge-button state, which honors `enforce_admins:false` admin bypass and is independent of required-check state — MERGEABLE is a false positive here.
  4. If the required context and the check-run name differ, bind the context to the job id via `tools/scripts/setup-branch-protection.mjs` (single source of truth) before claiming done.

Before claiming a GitHub branch-protection required status check is satisfied, verify via `gh pr view <n> --json mergeStateStatus` == CLEAN (or `commits/<sha>/check-runs[].name` equals a required `context` with `conclusion: success`), NOT via `gh pr view --json mergeable` — `mergeable` reports the merge-button state and honors `enforce_admins:false` admin bypass, so it returns MERGEABLE regardless of required-check state (a false-positive completion claim). description: Runtime context-injection is fragmented across overlapping surfaces with an undocumented division of labor and a duplicated, drift-prone source, which confuses the agent runtime about what is authoritative and where to get context. The surfaces: (1) SessionStart hook `.factory/hooks/loop-surface-inject.cjs` — push, cold, automatic at startup; per its header (:3) it claims to "inject loop_describe({tier:summary}) into context" but actually renders hardcoded LOCAL_DISCOVERABILITY_HINTS + LOCAL_PROCESS_HINTS copies plus a separately-spawned registry summary (the MCP server is spawned for counts but its hint strings are NOT trusted — security comment :14-16: "The server's discoverability_hints field is not trusted at render time. To update the hints, edit this file and commit"). (2) `loop_describe` MCP tool (loop-describe-tool.js) — pull, on-demand, tiered hot/warm/cold/summary; returns the CANONICAL buildDiscoverabilityHints()+buildProcessHints() (core/loop-introspect.js) PLUS dynamic `rules`/`tools`/`active_findings`/`loop_designs` read live from the registry. (3) `loop_get_instruction` MCP tool — pull, on-demand single-hint lookup by slug/index. (4) AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / `learning-loop` skill — static always-loaded steering prompt + prompt-author docs. Overlaps that confuse the runtime: (a) the SAME hints are delivered via two channels (push at SessionStart from a LOCAL copy, pull via loop_describe from the CANONICAL copy) with a parity test (cold-session-discoverability) enforcing they match — neither is "the" source, and editing one without the other drifts (this just caused a CI failure: rule promoted, canonical PROCESS_HINTS row added, LOCAL mirror missed). (b) Within loop_describe itself an agent-checklist rule appears in BOTH the dynamic `rules` field AND a hand-curated `process_hints` row; the H6 gate (loop-describe-tool.js:111-118) couples them by nagging "Add a hint referencing this rule to core/loop-introspect.js PROCESS_HINTS" rather than auto-generating — so one rule is represented in the registry entry, the dynamic rules field, a canonical hint row, a LOCAL hook hint row, and possibly AGENTS.md. (c) The stated principle (canonical/hook hint: "Each surface has a distinct role; do not duplicate content across them") is violated by design: the hook↔canonical hint duplication and the rule↔process_hints coupling ARE cross-surface duplication, kept because the hook deliberately mistrusts server hint strings at render time. (d) No documented division of labor between push (SessionStart) and pull (loop_describe / loop_get_instruction): an agent that already has the SessionStart hints does not know whether calling loop_describe warm tier adds anything (it does — dynamic rules/findings — but the hints block is the same content), and loop_get_instruction re-lookup of a hint already in the SessionStart injection is redundant. Recommendation: document the surface division of labor (push=cold-start fixed context, pull=current/deep context, on-demand=single re-fetch); make the trust boundary explicit and single-source the hints the hook DOES trust (e.g., the hook reads canonical via a node helper that imports the ESM core, or auto-generates process_hints rows for agent-checklist rules so promotion is one tool call not three); and reconcile the "do not duplicate across surfaces" principle with the hook's security-driven LOCAL copy (either trust the canonical at render with a signature/integrity check, or accept the duplication but make it mechanically enforced, not parity-tested). Related but distinct from meta-260715T2237Z-entry-relationships (that finding is about entry cross-ref storage/validation/retrieval; this is about the runtime context-INJECTION surfaces that deliver those entries to the agent).
resolution: Resolved by plans/260717-1826-unify-context-injection (Phase 1-4, completed 2026-07-17). Change-log: meta-260717T2006Z. Fixes: (a) LOCAL_* hint mirror deleted — factory hook renders canonical builders via direct core import; (b) MCP spawn probe killed — counts come from cheap core readers; (c) H6 ordering-gate nag deleted — rule-derived hint_text projection enforced at promotion time; (d) division of labor documented in docs/architecture.md § Context-Injection Division of Labor (push / pull-warm / pull-single / static); trust boundary explicit (hooks read core directly, no server-rendered strings cross trust). State-2 mechanism preserved (deterministic injection, agentic consumption).

description: runtime_state_record's `metadata` param (z.record(z.unknown())) accepts arbitrarily nested values, but array-typed values inside metadata were corrupted in transit on the npx-skills-mastra-roundtrip-2026-07-19 ledger row: pending_execution landed as 7-deep nested arrays containing a stray closing-tag artifact, and the row's fingerprint was computed over the corrupt payload. runtime-state.jsonl is append-only, so there is no in-place repair path — the corrected row had to be appended as a superseding entry (fingerprint sha256:0abcf89...). The same metadata channel remains open to this corruption class for any future array-valued metadata; either validate/sanitize nested arrays at the handler (reject or flatten with a warning) or document the strings-only contract on the tool description.

SessionStart steering injection is push-dependent and silently undelivered on lean provider profiles; transcript records injections that never reach the model (transcript ≠ wire). Measured: session b96b96c3-0808-4a40-8a2b-466b84a50975 (GLM-5.2 via CCS syn profile with CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC=1) recorded ~101KB of SessionStart/listing attachments but its first API call totalled 9,322 tokens with zero hint visibility (no hint references in thinking, no loop_describe call); same harness+hooks on the mm profile delivered a 67,890-token first call (session 7b63f076-b35d-4d53-9d1d-2732d78bbe39). Enforcement is delivery-independent and survived (hooks execute harness-side; gates need no prompt channel; MCP tool contracts re-deliver at invocation) — graceful degradation, task completed correctly. What degrades is state-1 steering (discovery rituals, process hints, canonical-tool preferences) with no error signal: (a) citizenship drift across profiles; (b) measurement corruption — "model ignored hint" vs "hint never sent" are behaviorally indistinguishable, so cross-profile compliance metrics are unsound without delivery tagging; (c) profile switches are user-initiated and invisible to the loop. Related but distinct locus: meta-260704T0959Z-orchestrator-session-read-the-full-326-line-source-of-tools (per-call behavioral unreliability of prose channels; this finding is per-session structural delivery — neither subsumes the other). Remediation direction (plans/reports/debug-260719-1524-ak-cook-context-attribution.md §Addendum 2): pointer-not-payload steering via UserPromptSubmit hook (verified survivor on the lean path), payloads pull-based (loop_describe / .claude/session-context.json), per-profile delivery attestation via one wire capture, session tagging by active profile. Exact strip point (harness flag vs synthetic.new proxy transform) unverified without a wire capture.

description: Central-skills Phase 3 chose a runtime-state ledger-event hand-off as the Q4 npx-unavailable fallback: "whichever sandbox can run npx executes the round-trip, writes a ledger-event, and Phase 3 reads it back to confirm." Three coupled failures: (a) no sandbox is constructed or selected — a static plan cannot pick which executor runs npx; (b) the report-back loop is untested — no test asserts the round-trip criterion flips on a row; (c) the same-id "correction" append at runtime-state.jsonl:24 cannot supersede the 08:13:00 row at any id-keyed `find` site, because findDispatchRow (tools/learning-loop-mastra/tools/handlers/meta-state-dispatch-finding-tool.js:45-50) returns the first match. The schema at schemas/runtime-state.schema.json:15-18 defines id as ^[a-z0-9-]+$ only — no uniqueness, no supersession field; same-id append-correction masquerades as supersession via metadata.supersedes_fingerprint but no read site honors it. This is the escape-hatch-abuse subtype: the writer's "I corrected it" assumption collides with the reader's first-match-wins. The inversion: a static plan cannot "read runtime-state back and mark a criterion met" — only a test does. Resolution: drop the hand-off from plans/260719-1428-central-skills-management Phase 3; gate the npx-round-trip criterion on the F6 hash test (phase-03 step 17: real npx when available; hash-recompute-and-compare when not); decouple F11/F12 from the ledger row to plain presence + byte-identity tests; strike the two corrupt same-id rows from runtime-state.jsonl. See plan 260720-1404.

The override path is also broken: mastra_gate_override requires a *promoted* rule_id, and "runtime-state" is a simple-glob rule in BOUND_ARTIFACTS, not promoted in WRITE_GATE_RULES — verified empirically: gate_override({rule_id: "runtime-state"}) returns `error: unknown rule_id: runtime-state` from gate-override-tool.js:24 (same shape as the schemas override error). Surfaces during one-time cleanup of corrupt same-id rows (observed 2026-07-20 during plan 260720-1404 Phase 3a: two same-id ledger-event rows needed a manual operator strike because no sanctioned path existed). Resolution mirrors 260720-1112 Phase 2 (option 1: preflight-delegation): migrate runtime-state from a simple-glob block in BOUND_ARTIFACTS to a preflight-delegating rule in WRITE_GATE_RULES; evaluateRuntimeStatePreflight checks for a `.loop-preflight-runtime-state` marker; gate_mark_preflight({surface:"runtime-state"}) unlocks writes for 30 minutes. Alternative (option 2: row-CRUD MCP): add a meta_state_runtime_state_strike MCP tool that takes an id and deletes that specific row(s) with a 1-step gate override audit (similar to meta_state_archive), gated on LOOP_SESSION_MODE=live. Either fix unblocks future row-CRUD without operator manual bypass. Out of scope for plan 260720-1404 (scope-creep risk; only the strike happened, not the fix). See plan 260720-1404.

npx skills CLI clobbers skills-lock.json on every `npx skills add`/`update`, rewriting the mastra entry to its native format (sourceType:"github", computedHash) and dropping the extended trust-anchor fields (external:true, delivery, targets, maturity, hash). The contract's manifest-driven exclusion (contract.js listLoopMaintainedSkills) reads external:true to exclude mastra; with the field dropped, mastra is enumerated and the F10 parity test fails. Verified empirically 2026-07-20: `npx skills add mastra-ai/skills --copy` and `npx skills update mastra` both clobbered the manifest. Workaround: after every npx add/update, restore the extended mastra entry (hash = sha256 of the detected SKILL.md) then `pnpm skills:sync`. A self-healing `pnpm skills:normalize` step is the sustainable fix (tracked follow-up).

Resolves meta-260720T1451Z.

description: Transport diversification to a CLI is a deferred decision, now gated on three triggers (T1/T2/T3). The loop realizes the runtime-contract (docs/runtime-contract.md) via a single transport — MCP+hooks, wired for 3 runtimes; shell-hook-only and library-import are named but unwired. Per plans/reports/from-root-cause-to-transport-decision-260711-0011-mcp-stateless-adapter-vs-cli-report.md, the CLI swap (Option C) was deferred under two triggers: T1 chronic lifecycle pain (stale-PID kills, not a one-off 22h server); T2 acceptable ergonomics loss (agent tolerates Bash-as-tool-channel, no zod, bash-gate allowlisting the loop's own CLI). A 2026-07-21 ak:predict session (standpoint: plan 260720-1955 context-size + delivery-observability) added a third trigger on a new axis: T3 chronic silent-undelivery of push-steering that a pull-based CLI would structurally prevent — distinct from T1/T2's statefulness axis. T3 is only measurable once plan 260720-1955 ships its delivery classifier (delivery-<sessionId> rows in runtime-state.jsonl); until then T3 is opinion (same shape as 260711-0011 Unresolved Q1). No CLI wiring until at least one trigger fires with evidence. Contract clarification (shell-hook-only = read-only) is recorded on meta-260711T0125Z; this finding tracks the wiring gate. Ship plan 260720-1955 first; re-evaluate T3 against classifier data post-plan.

docs/runtime-contract.md has a transport-agnostic contract gap between Capability 3 (record routing) and the shell-hook-only transport description. Capability 3 states: "The runtime never writes `records/**`, `meta-state.jsonl`, or `runtime-state.jsonl` directly. All writes go through the loop's tools, so the registry's invariants (4-kind union, status lifecycle, grounding fingerprints) hold regardless of which runtime produced the record." The shell-hook-only transport description states: "only the gate hooks are wired (no MCP tool surface); the runtime relies on the loop's file-based records **without a tool channel**." These two statements tension: if there is no tool channel, how do writes satisfy Capability 3? Two readings surfaced in plans/reports/from-root-cause-to-transport-decision-260711-0011-mcp-stateless-adapter-vs-cli-report.md §7: (a) shell-hook-only is read-only participation (capability 3 is unsatisfiable under that transport, contract should say so); (b) a CLI counts as a tool channel (just not MCP), so the contract should name it and wiring a CLI would *define* the shell-hook-only transport rather than merely wire it. Unresolved since plan 260711-0011 (Unresolved Question #2 in the prior decision report). This is a pre-existing L2 design question; the current plan 260711-0030 (stateless MCP adapter) does NOT wire shell-hook-only and explicitly defers the question. Decision needed before any non-MCP transport is wired (library-import or CLI). Deferred to follow-up plan per the prior report's recommendation. Scope: contract clarification + optional transport definition.

**Consensus (2026-07-21 ak:predict session):** adopt reading (a) — shell-hook-only is **read-only participation**; Capability 3 is unsatisfiable under that transport and the contract should state so. A CLI would *define* a write-capable tool channel (non-MCP), named as a future option, not wired now. Resolution is **docs-only**: update `docs/runtime-contract.md` Capability 3 + transport mapping so shell-hook-only carries no write path, and a CLI (if ever wired) is the minimal write-capable tool channel. No transport wiring in this resolution. The CLI-swap *wiring* decision is tracked separately in `meta-260721T0809Z-transport-diversification-to-a-cli-is-a-deferred-decision-no` under a three-trigger gate (T1 chronic lifecycle pain; T2 acceptable ergonomics loss; T3 chronic silent-undelivery a pull-CLI structurally prevents; plan 260720-1955's delivery classifier instruments T3). This finding resolves when the docs clarification commits.

SessionStart steering injection is push-dependent and silently undelivered on lean provider profiles; transcript records injections that never reach the model (transcript ≠ wire). Measured: session b96b96c3-0808-4a40-8a2b-466b84a50975 (GLM-5.2 via CCS syn profile with CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC=1) recorded ~101KB of SessionStart/listing attachments but its first API call totalled 9,322 tokens with zero hint visibility (no hint references in thinking, no loop_describe call); same harness+hooks on the mm profile delivered a 67,890-token first call (session 7b63f076-b35d-4d53-9d1d-2732d78bbe39). Enforcement is delivery-independent and survived (hooks execute harness-side; gates need no prompt channel; MCP tool contracts re-deliver at invocation) — graceful degradation, task completed correctly. What degrades is state-1 steering (discovery rituals, process hints, canonical-tool preferences) with no error signal: (a) citizenship drift across profiles; (b) measurement corruption — "model ignored hint" vs "hint never sent" are behaviorally indistinguishable, so cross-profile compliance metrics are unsound without delivery tagging; (c) profile switches are user-initiated and invisible to the loop. Related but distinct locus: meta-260704T0959Z-orchestrator-session-read-the-full-326-line-source-of-tools (per-call behavioral unreliability of prose channels; this finding is per-session structural delivery — neither subsumes the other). Remediation direction (plans/reports/debug-260719-1524-ak-cook-context-attribution.md §Addendum 2): pointer-not-payload steering via UserPromptSubmit hook (verified survivor on the lean path), payloads pull-based (loop_describe / .claude/session-context.json), per-profile delivery attestation via one wire capture, session tagging by active profile. Exact strip point (harness flag vs synthetic.new proxy transform) unverified without a wire capture.
resolution: Remediation realized in plan 260720-1955: SessionStart steering is delivered as a pull pointer (slug — suggestion projection, combined hook stdout 13,088 → 5,120 chars) plus a once-per-session inbound-gate pull pointer (V2, suppress-token-gated), not a silent push. Delivery is now observable: tools/scripts/delivery-classify.mjs writes delivery-<sessionId> ledger rows (full/lean/unknown, content-hash re-classify V5) to runtime-state.jsonl, queryable via runtime_state_read; delivered-token metric = input + cache_read + cache_creation (H7). Pull payloads intact (sidecar shape hash unchanged, *_source=core). Manifest-tool wire slimmed to 39,905 B (≤40 kB) via JIT branch contracts + shared field glossary. Verification: plans/260720-1955-.../reports/verification-260720-final.md. syn-profile forensics recorded as documented-degradation (transcript absent).

description: Transport diversification to a CLI: gate SATISFIED via T3 (operator decision 2026-07-21); Phase 1 read-only slice approved. Original gate: no CLI wiring until >=1 of T1/T2/T3 fires with evidence. T3 evidence-definition revision: the evidence base is the incident history, not classifier lean rows — measured silent-undelivery incident meta-260719T2120Z (session b96b96c3: ~101KB recorded SessionStart attachments, 9,322-token first call, zero hint visibility, transcript != wire, found by manual forensics), unverified closure on the incident profile (resolution records syn-profile forensics as documented-degradation, transcript absent), cross-locus recurrence of push-channels-born-silent (meta-260704T0959Z, meta-260715T2300Z). Agent runtime embeds plan IDs, phase numbers, and finding codes in stable code artifacts (code comments, test names, migration names, commit messages) despite the operator's global rule `review-audit-self-decision.md` § "Stable Code Artifacts" forbidding exactly that ("Do not put plan IDs, phase numbers, audit labels, or finding codes in code comments, migration names, test names, or commit messages. Explain the invariant or behavior directly.").

Self-reinforcing: an established codebase convention PRECEDES the rule (~42 instances across core/ + mastra/ — bound-artifacts.js:5/93, hint-renderer.js:3, surfaces.js:50/69/126, evaluate-write-gate.js:92/96/100/185, gate-logic.js:35, check-grounding.js:23/35, path-containment.js:30, inbound-state.js:9, file-readers.js:10, placement.yaml rows 19/22/46/77/86), and the development-rules baseline "match surrounding code" causes every new feature to propagate it. Most recent recurrence: the CLI-transport phase-1 slice (bin/loop.mjs:2, core/schema-normalize.js:8, mastra/create-loop-tool.js:18, core/placement.yaml:86, all 3 new test-file headers) embeds "Phase N of plan 260721-1933" — 7 instances.

Harm: plan IDs are ephemeral lineage; code comments are stable artifacts. Embedding them couples stable code to transient process state — plan dirs get archived/renamed, leaving dangling references that fail no test. It also contradicts an explicit operator rule, eroding rule credibility.

Alternative commenting convention: describe the INVARIANT or BEHAVIOR directly, not plan lineage. If a cross-reference is needed, link by concept/topic (e.g. "see docs/runtime-contract.md § Transport mapping") not by plan ID. Put plan lineage in plan docs, cook reports, and git history — never in code.

Prevention: (a) promote an agent-checklist rule restating the operator's "Stable Code Artifacts" rule so agent runtimes see it at session start; (b) add a regression test that greps core/+mastra/+bin/ source for plan-ID comment patterns and fails on NEW matches — but it MUST exclude legitimate test-fixture usages (plan paths used as test INPUT data or evidence_journal fixture values, e.g. evaluate-write-gate.test.js:259, meta-state-g8-supersede.test.js:43) and docs (README.md). Comprehensive removal is a separate hand-edit sweep (rewrite each comment to describe the invariant), NOT a mechanical find-replace.

title: Plan-ID-free stable code artifacts — removal sweep, alternative convention, prevention test
description:
Resolve finding meta-260721T2300Z-agent-runtime-embeds-plan-ids-phase-numbers-and-finding-code in three coordinated steps.

STEP 1 — Alternative convention (codify first so the sweep has a target): code comments describe the invariant/behavior directly; cross-references link by concept (docs/ section or module name), never by plan ID/phase number/finding code. Plan lineage lives in plan docs, cook reports, and git history only. Add this to docs/code-standards.md as the "Stable code artifacts" section, quoting the operator's global rule verbatim.

STEP 2 — Comprehensive removal sweep (hand-edit, NOT mechanical find-replace): for each of the ~42 established + 7 new instances, rewrite the comment to describe the invariant. Examples: `// Phase 3 of plans/260707-0114-loop-skill-layer-prerequisite/plan.md.` → `// Bound-artifact registry: the trust root for which generated files the gate may touch.`; `// removed by migration in plan 260607-dual-field-schema-unification` → `// Legacy dual-field shape is no longer produced; the single canonical field is ...`. Each rewrite requires reading the surrounding code to name the actual invariant. DO NOT touch legitimate test-fixture usages (plan paths as test INPUT data or evidence_journal fixture values — evaluate-write-gate.test.js:259, meta-state-g8-supersede.test.js:43) or docs (README.md).

STEP 3 — Prevention regression test: add __tests__/stable-artifacts-no-plan-ids.test.js that greps core/+mastra/+bin/ source (excluding __tests__ fixture lines and .md docs) for plan-ID comment patterns — /\b(plan|plans)\/\d{6}-/, /Phase \d+ of (plan|plans)/, /plan \d{6}-\d{4}/ — and fails on any match. Seed it with a snapshot of the CURRENT matched lines as the allowlist baseline (so the sweep removes entries incrementally and the test locks the floor: no NEW matches). Once the sweep completes, the allowlist is empty and the test forbids all plan-ID comments. This is the regression that prevents agent runtime recurrence.

Rollback: each step is independently revertible; the test is additive; the sweep is comment-only (no behavior change).

Idempotent: re-running the sweep is a no-op once the allowlist is empty.

description:
Promoting an agent-checklist rule is a manual 4-location cascade with no automated coupling, and 3 of those locations hardcode registry counts that are trivially derivable. This just caused the PR-#73 CI failure (rule-no-plan-ids-in-stable-code-artifacts).

The 4 locations an agent-checklist rule promotion MUST touch:
1. `meta-state.jsonl` — the rule entry (id, enforcement, pattern_type=agent-checklist, pattern, hint_text). Done by `meta_state_promote_rule` MCP tool. This is the ONLY location the tool writes.
2. `core/hint-registry.js` — a hand-mirrored `HINT_REGISTRY` entry with `derived_from_rule === rule.id`, a slug, `kind: "process"`, `text: ""` (renderer resolves prose from `rule.hint_text`), and a one-line `suggestion`. The promote tool CANNOT write this (it writes the registry, not source code). Missing it fails `legacy-mcp/consult-checklist-process-hints-coverage.test.js` (the Phase-3 invariant: every active agent-checklist rule MUST have a matching `derived_from_rule` registry entry). This is the failure that broke CI.
3. `__tests__/hint-registry.test.cjs` — hardcodes `process` slug count (10), `buildProcessHints` length (10), `listHints({kind:'process'})` length (10), and total (26).
4. `__tests__/hint-renderer.test.cjs` + `__tests__/rule-derived-process-hints.test.cjs` — hardcode total hints (26), degraded-skip warnings count (8), sidecar `process_hints` length (10), mcp-warm array length (26), provenance rows (26), and "8 rule-derived process rows at positions 2-10".

Root smell: the numeric counts (26/10/8) are PURE DERIVATIONS of `HINT_REGISTRY.length`, `.filter(e => e.derived_from_rule).length`, and `.filter(e => e.kind === 'process').length` — yet they are hand-locked as literals. Adding one rule-derived hint bumps 26->27, 10->11, 8->9 and requires hand-editing all 3 test files. The agent must know to do this; nothing enforces or reminds them. The slug-list + ordering assertions in these tests ARE legitimate (they catch real drift) and should stay hardcoded — only the pure-numeric counts should be derived.

Two coupled gaps:
A. The promote tool does not (cannot) create the companion `HINT_REGISTRY` entry — the agent must hand-edit `core/hint-registry.js`. No reminder, no checklist hint (ironically, the rule-promotion workflow itself lacks the agent-checklist treatment).
B. The count-locking tests hardcode derivable numbers, turning a 1-location registry addition into a 4-file manual cascade.

Remediation direction:
- Gap A: either (i) a companion `register_hint_for_rule` MCP tool (or a `meta_state_promote_rule` option) that appends the `HINT_REGISTRY` entry to `core/hint-registry.js` from the rule id + hint_text (slug = rule-id minus 'rule-' prefix; suggestion = first sentence of hint_text; derived_from_rule = rule.id), so promotion is 1 MCP call; or (ii) an agent-checklist rule + regression test that forbids an active agent-checklist rule without a `HINT_REGISTRY` entry (the coverage test already does this — so the real fix is making the promote path produce the entry, not just flag its absence).
- Gap B: replace the hardcoded numeric counts in the 3 test files with `HINT_REGISTRY`-derived values (`registry.HINT_REGISTRY.length`, `.filter(derived_from_rule).length`, `.filter(kind==='process').length`). Keep the slug-list + ordering + per-partition-budget assertions hardcoded (those are the real signal). After this, adding a rule-derived hint is: 1 promote call + 1 slug-list append (the legitimate drift signal) — not a 4-file cascade.

Evidence: the PR-#73 CI failure (run 29849986465, `test` workflow) was exactly gap A (missing HINT_REGISTRY entry); the 9 follow-on test failures from the fix were exactly gap B (hardcoded counts). Both gaps are reproducible by promoting any new agent-checklist rule.

title: Single-location agent-checklist rule promotion — derive hint-registry counts, auto-register the hint entry
description:
Close the two coupled gaps from meta-260722T0001Z so promoting an agent-checklist rule is a 1-location change (the rule) + at most 1 slug-list append (the legitimate drift signal), not a 4-file manual cascade.

STEP 1 — Derive the count locks (Gap B). In the 3 count-locking test files, replace hardcoded numeric counts with HINT_REGISTRY-derived values:
- `hint-registry.test.cjs`: `proc.length` and `listHints({kind:'process'}).length` -> `registry.HINT_REGISTRY.filter(e => e.kind === 'process').length`; `listHints({kind:undefined}).length` -> `registry.HINT_REGISTRY.length`; `buildProcessHints` length -> same process count. KEEP the slug-list `deepStrictEqual` (legitimate drift signal) and the standalone-rows-position assertions.
- `hint-renderer.test.cjs`: `provenance.length` / `slugs.size` / mcp-warm `arr.length` -> `registry.HINT_REGISTRY.length` (27 today, derived); degraded-skip `warnings.length` -> `registry.HINT_REGISTRY.filter(e => e.derived_from_rule).length`; sidecar `process_hints.length` -> process count. KEEP per-partition budget + 2-partition-shape + slug/marker presence assertions.
- `rule-derived-process-hints.test.cjs`: `derived.length` -> `.filter(derived_from_rule).length`; KEEP the standalone-position assertions (0 and 8) and `standalone.length === 2` (the 2 standalone rows are a real invariant, not a derivation of the rule count).
After this step, adding a rule-derived hint changes ONLY the slug-list expected array in `hint-registry.test.cjs` (the real signal that a new slug landed in the right place) — the numeric tests auto-track the registry.

STEP 2 — Auto-register the hint entry on promotion (Gap A). Add a companion code-writing step to the agent-checklist promotion path. Two viable shapes (pick in plan):
- (i) `meta_state_promote_rule` gains a `register_hint: true` default for agent-checklist that appends the `HINT_REGISTRY` entry to `core/hint-registry.js` from the rule: slug = rule_id without `rule-` prefix; kind = 'process'; text = '' (renderer resolves from hint_text); suggestion = first sentence of hint_text (<= ~160 chars); derived_from_rule = rule_id. The tool already writes meta-state.jsonl; extending it to also edit the one source file is the single-point fix. Gate the source edit through the existing write-gate (`core/hint-registry.js` is loop-source, not a preflight-gated path, so it's writable).
- (ii) A standalone `register_hint_for_rule` MCP tool the agent calls right after promote, taking rule_id + optional slug/suggestion override. Lower-risk (no change to promote semantics) but 2 calls instead of 1.
Either way: the coverage test `consult-checklist-process-hints-coverage.test.js` already enforces the entry exists, so the fix is verifiable — a promoted rule with no hint entry fails CI until step 2 lands, then passes automatically.

STEP 3 — Prevention regression. Add a test that promotes a throwaway agent-checklist rule in a tmpdir registry + tmpdir hint-registry copy and asserts the hint entry was created with `derived_from_rule === rule.id` (locks step 2's behavior). Plus, after step 1, add a test that asserts NONE of the 3 count-locking test files contain a bare numeric literal for the registry size (grep guard: `/\b===?\s*(26|10|8|27|11|9)\b/` in those files' count assertions -> fail), so a future edit can't silently re-hardcode the counts.

Rollback: step 1 is test-only (revertible); step 2 is a tool change (revert the register_hint option); step 3 is additive tests. No behavior change to existing rules (the 8 existing agent-checklist rules already have hint entries; deriving counts yields the same numbers today: 27/11/9).

Idempotent: once step 1 lands, re-deriving counts is a no-op; once step 2 lands, re-promoting an already-promoted rule is a no-op (hint entry already exists).

runtime-state.jsonl has two coupled maintenance gaps that let stale/non-actionable rows accumulate and pollute the loop's own ledger view. Surfaced by the inbound gate reporting 15 stale vnstock runtime-state observations alongside 12 meta-state-tools ones — vnstock is a vendored example surface in this template, not a system the operator is running, yet its ledger-events persist and crowd out the loop's own records.

GAP 1 — no versioned dedup (unlike meta-state). meta-state.jsonl is versioned-append: each id can have multiple version lines and reads collapse by max_by(version) (meta_state_list's projection). runtime-state.jsonl has NO version field: the row schema is {affected_system, kind, id, source_ref, value, delta, timestamp, metadata, fingerprint} where `fingerprint` is a SHA-256 row-INTEGRITY hash (detects post-write mutation), NOT an idempotency/version key. `appendLedgerEvent` (core/runtime-state.js) ALWAYS appends; `readRuntimeStateRows` returns every row; `runtime_state_read` filters then `.slice(0, limit=20)` with no per-id max-by-version projection. Re-recording the same ledger-event `id` (e.g. a budget-state row for the same source_ref, or a re-dispatch) creates duplicate rows, and reads return up to 20 stale copies of the same logical event. Evidence: core/runtime-state.js readRuntimeStateRows + appendLedgerEvent + the runtime-state-read handler's filter-then-slice (no version/dedup). Fix direction: add a `version` field to runtime-state rows (or reuse id+source_ref as the dedup key) and have `runtime_state_read` collapse to max_by(version) per id (mirroring meta-state), so the sidecar stores history but reads return the current state, not N stale copies.

GAP 2 — no way to stop/pause runtime tracking for a surface. Both writers (`runtime_state_record`, preflight-gated; `meta_state_dispatch_finding`, live-gated) append unconditionally — there is no per-surface tracking toggle, allowlist, or pause mechanism. When the operator is building the LOOP (not vnstock), vnstock's ledger-events still append (from vendored product code / hooks / earlier work) and pollute runtime-state.jsonl, mixing non-actionable vnstock noise with the loop's own meta-state-tools / runtime-state ledger-events that actually drive the self-learning loop. There is no MCP tool or config to say "stop tracking vnstock." Fix direction: a per-surface tracking gate — either (a) a tracked-surfaces allowlist / paused-surfaces denylist config that both writers consult before appending, or (b) a `runtime_state_pause`/`runtime_state_resume` MCP tool that flips a surface's tracking flag. Either keeps runtime-state.jsonl focused on the surfaces the operator actually runs.

Combined harm: the inbound gate's stale-observation scan and `runtime_state_read` both surface vnstock noise, eroding signal on the loop's own ledger-events; the sidecar grows monotonically with no dedup or stop. The two gaps compound — gap 2 keeps vnstock rows flowing in, gap 1 keeps every stale version of them.

Scope note: this is a template-repo concern — vnstock is the vendored example product; the operator is building the loop. The fix should make "I'm not running surface X" expressible and keep that surface's rows out of the loop's ledger view.

resolution: Write-capable CLI shipped + dogfooded on .claude. Plan 260722-1343 delivered: (1) bin/loop.mjs executes every CLI_TOOLS member (7 reads + 16 portable mutation handlers) via the same handler/R2-gate/lock path as MCP; (2) self-footgun guard in meta_state_promote_rule activation rejects regex patterns matching canonical CLI invocation shapes (core/cli-self-match.js); (3) exit 1 splits into recognized-rejection JSON vs InternalError JSON (distinct shapes for agent recovery policy), exit 2 stays for usage/caller-config; (4) --schema pulls the normalized input schema on demand (no SessionStart re-injection); (5) LOOP_RECORDS_VIA_CLI=1 drops every CLI_TOOLS member from MCP, leaving workflow/storage/allowlist/audit + auxiliary read-ish tools as the irreducible residue. .claude migrated from LOOP_READS_VIA_CLI=1 to LOOP_RECORDS_VIA_CLI=1 after T2 write-path evidence accrued (write-parity tests green, change-log entry meta-260722T1433Z recorded via the CLI itself, exit-code contract verified). docs/runtime-contract.md L24/L26/L42 now name the write-capable CLI as a wired transport, framed as an example realization of Capability 3 on a non-MCP transport so L5 transport-agnostic framing stays intact. .factory / .mastracode generalize by setting the same flag after their own dogfood — separate evidence gate, not claimed by this closure.

description: The write-capable CLI transport's context-savings dogfood check is ad-hoc, not systematized, and its results are not recorded in the loop's own ledger. During the review of commit ae431ef the savings were measured once by hand (31.8 KB of dropped MCP record-surface tool defs vs a 1.91 KB SessionStart banner = 29.9 KB / 94% saved per session start) using a throwaway script that was deleted after use. Three gaps: (1) no reproducible check — the measurement lives in no committed script, so it cannot re-run or track drift over time; (2) no runtime-state record — each measurement should be a runtime_state_record ledger-event (affected_system=runtime-state) so the loop's own ledger tracks context-size wins across sessions and surfaces regressions (a new MCP tool re-injected into context, or a sketch/banner re-bloat, would shrink the savings and be detectable from the ledger); (3) no regression guard tying the measurement to the existing <2 KiB banner budget lock (cli-sessionstart-banner.test.js) and the CLI_TOOLS drift guard. Systemize: a committed script (e.g. tools/scripts/measure-cli-context.mjs) or a vitest check that, for the current CLI_TOOLS set, computes dropped-def bytes vs banner bytes, asserts the banner stays under budget, and emits a runtime_state_record row per run. Upstream context: change-log entry meta-260722T1433Z (write-capable CLI landed) and resolved finding meta-260721T0809Z (transport diversification). This finding closes the dogfood-evidence loop by making the context savings measured-and-recorded, not asserted-once. No evidence_code_ref: the measurement system does not exist yet, so mechanism_check is false by default (gap finding, not a grounded one).

description: Six portable workflow tools are CLI-capable in principle but stay on MCP under LOOP_RECORDS_VIA_CLI=1 because their re-homing is blocked on external prerequisites: run_workflow_classify_prompt, run_workflow_prepare_runtime_request, run_workflow_self_improvement, run_workflow_intentional_skip, run_workflow_report_phase_status, run_workflow_runtime_probe. The drift test (cli-write-tool-set-drift.test.js) tags these MCP_RESIDUE entries with reason=deferred-rehoming per docs/runtime-contract.md L2 § Transport capability. Prerequisites per the workflow audit (plans/reports/ak-problem-solving-260722-2125-workflow-tool-transport-home-audit.md): (U-Q1) unwrap contract — createLoopWorkflow (tools/learning-loop-mastra/core/create-loop-workflow.js) normalizes each workflow via attachParityJSONSchema + envelope strip; re-homing must preserve that schema normalization (shared pre-handler or per-tool, not duplicate); (U-Q2) resolveRoot wiring — workflow_generate_prompt references blueprints under the learning-loop-mcp subtree, so the CLI path must resolve roots correctly across the wire (create-loop-workflow / workflow-generate-prompt-tool.js); (P-Q2) ordering-enforcement prerequisite — multi-step workflow execution must keep ordering enforceable via gate-observed (not agent-asserted) step-success; (Sec-F9) the convertWorkflowsToTools opt-out gap at server.js:187 has no parallel RECORDS_VIA_CLI branch — re-homing a run_workflow_* to CLI requires either a parallel opt-out there or the tool stays dual-surface. Precondition for any unwrap code: a separate evidence-driven plan that names the unwrap contract (U-Q1), the resolveRoot path (U-Q2), the ordering mechanism (P-Q2), and the server.js opt-out patch. L2 transport-capability criterion cited via change-log meta-260723T0811Z-docs-runtime-contract-md; this finding is the durable deferral record so the decision does not live only in the audit report. No re-homing code committed in plan 260722-2147.

Stale references: skills/coordination-gate/SKILL.md lines 25, 33, 46, 51, 53 (index_extract, index_validate, capability_generate, index_search, index_update_claim); tools/learning-loop-mastra/tools/handlers/references/tool-selection-guide.md line 103 (capability_generate). WORKFLOW_REGISTRY.recommended_tools was vacated to [] in plan 260722-2147 Phase 5 because the names are dead refs; the registry is now coherent. The skill + tool-selection-guide docs are bound artifacts with their own write-gate and out of scope for a separate skills-hygiene pass. Resolution = a follow-up skills-hygiene plan that audits agent-facing docs against the live manifest, removes or rewrites the deleted-tool references, and emits a change-log. No code in this plan is changed by the existence of this finding; the drift-test gap is closed by Phase 5 regardless. U-Q3 (audit unresolved-question) is the upstream context.

workflow_generate_prompt returned {error:true,message:Blueprint file not found} for EVERY call because its BLUEPRINTS map pointed at tools/learning-loop-mcp/references/*.md — a directory removed when the learning-loop-mcp package folded into learning-loop-mastra and references/ relocated under tools/handlers/ (commits 5788890 + b458f1b + 89fb6f2). The real files live at tools/learning-loop-mastra/tools/handlers/references/. The handler try/catch swallowed the ENOENT and returned a soft error, so the breakage was silent and untested (no test invoked workflow_generate_prompt with a real blueprint arg — only set-membership assertions existed). Fix: BLUEPRINTS repointed to the real location; regression test workflow-generate-prompt-tool.test.js pins that every blueprint category resolves a skeleton. Residual: the tool now works under the loop repo root but NOT under a non-loop runtime root (LOOP_READS_VIA_CLI=1 with GATE_ROOT=product repo) — the blueprints live in the loop package, so cross-root resolution (U-Q2) remains unresolved. workflow_generate_prompt is therefore reclassified CLI_READ_TOOLS -> MCP_RESIDUE deferred-rehoming (consistent with the portable-six finding meta-260723T0813Z) until a dedicated re-homing plan handles cross-root blueprint resolution. Discovered during the post-merge review of plan 260722-2147.

Aged findings (no verification.steps) cannot be re-grounded by meta_state_re_verify: the handler returns re_verified:false reason:no_verification_steps when entry.verification.steps is empty (meta-state-re-verify-tool.js:44-46), but 17 age-stale findings (e.g. meta-260614T1236Z-..., meta-260714T1248Z-...) are still grounded with hash_match:true — the operator has no canonical way to re-stamp last_verified_at without first authoring per-finding verification steps. Cold-tier test bumped its age-stale cap (11→19) as the documented operator escape hatch, but the cap is a regression-detection forcing function, not a real fix; the registry will continue to age until either (a) each aged finding gains verification.steps (large per-finding work) or (b) a lighter operator action (e.g. meta_state_touch) is added that stamps last_verified_at when checkGrounding passes.
